name: Anthony Iannarino
title: Sales Kickoff Speaker
bio:
Anthony is a highly respected international speaker, bestselling author, entrepreneur, and sales leader specializing in the complex business-to-business (B2B) sale. He is also a founder and managing partner of two closely-held, family-owned businesses in the staffing industry, leading both entities in strategic planning while growing sales. Anthony is best known for his work at The Sales Blog, which has helped him gain recognition as a top thought leader in sales strategy. He is also the designer of Level 4 Value Creation™ and Building Consensus, methodologies that help sales organizations achieve transformational, breakthrough results.

In each business, Anthony is focused on helping professionals reach their full potential. In 2007, while growing the sales force of his second staffing firm, he discovered a knack for coaching, and realized that he could make enduring contributions to a company’s sales culture. He began blogging about complex selling processes, and gravitated toward B2B companies facing challenges in sales force management and performance. His transition to professional speaking, consulting, and workshop facilitation led to the development of a trademarked methodology that has proven instrumental in helping sales organizations achieve revenue goals.

Throughout his career, Anthony has helped people in a wide variety of industries and markets think through and overcome their biggest business challenges. His greatest strength is in getting others to build consensus around what must change, and identify the resources within themselves that will drive positive results. Be it through the delivery of staffing solutions or sales strategies, Anthony is a trust builder who focuses on leading transformational conversations, those that create and sustain relationships of value. He is a natural mentor who brings the business acumen, situational knowledge, and experience to each engagement, and lays a solid foundation for future growth.

bio:
Chad Reid is a SaaS marketing leader passionate about scaling brands and driving growth through innovative marketing strategies. Currently VP of Marketing at Jotform, he specializes in building high-performing teams and sharpening product storytelling to create meaningful customer connections.

A seasoned speaker, Chad brings deep experience in demand generation, content marketing, and partnerships—all essential for driving inbound success in today’s fast-evolving digital landscape.
session_title: Tools for Growth: 4 Ways AI and Smart Forms Drive Revenue

name: Charlie Ungashick
bio: Charlie Ungashick joined Vimeo as CMO in 2024. He has over 20 years of experience leading global marketing teams at high-growth tech companies like WorkHuman, Applause (acquired by Vista Equity Partners) and PTC (NASDAQ:PTC). He also served as an adviser at Gem, a talent engagement platform. Charlie has previously held product, sales engineering, and IT roles at AIG, Novell, and SilverStream Software. Charlie holds a bachelor’s degree from Fordham University and a diploma with honors in economics from the Université de Paris Sorbonne.
session_title: NextGen Marketing: Reimagining Storytelling With AI Video

name: Charlène Lardy Vella
bio: Charlène is a passionate professor with a diverse background that led her from the theater stage to writing. After creating and managing her own digital marketing training and consulting company and leading projects at a HubSpot Solution Partner, she has joined the Academy to help each company take its projects to the next level.
session_title: Lab: Unifying Your Tech Stack With HubSpot

name: Chris Cunningham
bio: Chris Cunningham is Head of Social Marketing and a co-founder of ClickUp, where he built its content into a B2B powerhouse with over 1B impressions in 2024. Under his leadership, ClickUp grew to 1,000+ employees, raised $535M, and reached a $4B valuation—with standout campaigns like a Super Bowl ad. Previously, he was the youngest manager at Cvent and led $50M+ in sales at Beverly Media. Chris is a sought-after speaker on social media, brand, and business growth.
session_title: Supercharge Demand Gen With Quality Leads on TikTok

name: Chris Riley
bio: Chris Riley is a technology advocate, founder, and Senior Manager in HubSpot’s Developer Relations team. With a background in AppDev, Site Reliability Engineering, and Developer Relations, Chris brings a unique perspective as a neurodiverse professional living with Autism Level 1, ADHD combined, and dyslexia. He has been a tireless advocate for reducing stigma and increasing awareness around neurodiversity in the workplace, encouraging organizations to embrace cognitive diversity as a competitive advantage.
session_title: Meetup: The Neurodivergent Edge in the Age of AI

name: Christian Kletzl
bio: Christian Kletzl is CEO and co-founder of UserGems, a pipeline generation software that helps teams boost pipeline and reduce churn by monitoring job changes of your most valuable buyers, then automating the next steps.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Choose an AI Outbound Agent That Works

name: Christian McKenzie
bio: Christian McKenzie is the Director of Lofty Ventures, a giver-centric community on a mission to connect visionary people and fuel entrepreneurship and economic development—especially in Chicago. She began her career as a retail buyer, curating products for over 250 Macy’s stores. Then she transitioned into tech with companies like Meta and Pear (acquired by CustomInk). Wanting to make a bigger impact, she shifted her focus from supporting brands to investing in them—using her passion for emerging ideas to champion the next generation of founders.
session_title: How to Get Your Business Venture Capital Ready

name: Christian Sibley
bio: Christian is a Product Lead in Commerce Hub at HubSpot. He joined HubSpot following the acquisition of Cacheflow in 2024, where he built out Cacheflow’s quoting experience, driven under a mission to help sellers close deals faster and create an experience that buyers love. He is now continuing that mission for HubSpot’s quoting experience as well as its applied-AI products, because time kills deals!
session_title: From Quote Chaos to Closed-Won: HubSpot CPQ in Action

name: Christina Garnett
bio:
Christina Garnett is a Fractional CCO, customer experience strategist, and the author of Transforming Customer–Brand Relationships (Kogan Page, 2025). Her work focuses on helping brands build emotional connection, customer advocacy, and long-term loyalty through intentional customer experience, community engagement, and brand storytelling.

Christina’s insights have been featured in Adweek, Forbes, PR Daily, and HubSpot Academy, and she has spoken at global industry events including INBOUND, CNX, and Digital Summit.
session_title: DEBATE: Why Founder-Led Brands Falter

name: Christine Cho
bio: Christine Cho is a senior product manager with years of building and launching B2B products, first at LinkedIn and now at HubSpot. She is currently focused on product innovation in the AI Agent space. Based in the SF Bay Area, she can usually be found rock climbing peaks in Yosemite.
session_title: Build Your First AI Agent in Breeze Studio

name: Christine Lee
bio: Christine was born in Hong Kong, but has lived all over the United States, and is now back in her home state of New Hampshire. Christine is passionate about empowering others to develop into all they're capable of, which is why she loves working at HubSpot. She has her masters in clinical mental health counseling and is also a practicing therapist. When not working, you can probably find her drinking coffee and hanging out with her dog, Dobby.
session_title: Lab: HubSpot Email Marketing Toolkit for the AI Era

name: Christine Maxey
bio: Christine is the Director of Revenue Operations at Groq where she uses a combination of AI, analytics, and orchestration to streamline go-to-market execution, improve cross-functional efficiency, and drive sustained revenue growth. Before joining Groq, Christine held operations, customer success and solutions consulting roles at LeanData, Tackle.io, Pivotal, and Sun Microsystems.
session_title: HubSpot for Marketing. Different CRM. 1 Seamless GTM Motion.

name: Claire Ebben
bio: Claire Ebben is a Director of Product Marketing at Qualified, the agentic marketing platform with the world's #1 AI SDR agent, Piper. Prior to Qualified, Claire led Sales Cloud Market Strategy and worked as an Account Executive at Salesforce.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Let AI Agents Run Your Event Outreach

name: Co-founder and Co-CEO, Clozd
session_title: The Psychology Behind the 30-Second Commercial

name: Colby Kyes
bio: Colby has helped literally hundreds, nay, thousands of Wistia customers achieve their wildest video marketing dreams. As co-host of the hit Wistia series, “You Have It, Let’s Use It” he walks through practical steps to make the most of the Wistia tool set. As a CSM he coaches his customers on how to best leverage Wistia and its various integrations (including HubSpot). He’s excited to show the world that he does not live inside of a computer.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Turn Video Views Into Action With Wistia

name: Colin Gillingham
bio: Colin Gillingham leads HubSpot’s Conversational Enrichment product team, turning raw emails, calls, and chats into structured insights that move deals and resolve tickets faster. A product builder at the crossroads of AI and CRM, he also leads product for HubSpot's Data Enrichment engine. Colin’s passion is designing systems that let revenue teams spend less time digging for data and more time acting on it.
session_title: Conversational Enrichment: The Self Generating CRM

name: Colin Johnson
bio: Colin Johnson is the Senior Manager of CRM at Youth Enrichment Brands. With a passion for creative problem-solving, he uses HubSpot in innovative ways to connect sales, marketing, and ops. He’s all about turning CRM complexity into clear, high-impact experiences.
session_title: Enterprise Marketing Principles for Personalized Experiences

name: Colin Jost
bio: Colin Jost is a writer, performer, and author best known for his work on “Saturday Night Live.” Jost started writing for “SNL” in 2005 and has been the co-anchor of the show’s “Weekend Update” since 2014.
session_title: INBOUND After Hours

name: Collette King
bio: Collette King is a product lead in the Customer Retention and Growth product group in Service Hub. Her team builds functionality for proactive post-sale processes within HubSpot. Having been at HubSpot since 2019, Collette has worked in the Ecosystem and Developer product groups prior to joining Service Hub in 2023. She holds a BA from Tufts University.
session_title: Inside Service Hub: Building a Unified Post-Sales Journey

name: Courtney Sembler
bio: As Senior Director of HubSpot Academy, I’m passionate about education and inbound marketing, shaping the customer education landscape. I excel at stakeholder collaboration, adapting to evolving global landscapes, and developing top-tier talent. My expertise spans inbound marketing and scaling educational programs. I’m a visionary educator championing the transformative power of inbound marketing through educational content and credentials, contributing to individual and organizational growth globally.
session_title: ENCORE: Everboarding: The Modern Approach to Onboarding

name: Cristina Criddle
bio: Cristina Criddle writes about artificial intelligence for the FT and is based in San Francisco. She won a San Francisco Press Award in 2024 and gold at the 2023 MHP Group 30ToWatch Journalism awards for her technology reporting. Previously, she was a technology reporter at the BBC, where she presented on TV, radio and podcasts, as well as produced the documentary The Instagram Effect.
session_title: How AI Will Change Video

name: Crystal King
bio: Crystal King is a Senior Professor for HubSpot Academy, focusing on social media and AI strategy. Crystal has led global social media and community programs for companies such as Pegasystems, Keurig, CA Technologies, and Sybase. She has taught classes in writing, creativity, and social media at Harvard Extension School, Boston University, Mass College of Art, and UMass Boston. Crystal holds an M.A. in Critical and Creative Thinking from UMass Boston. She is also a novelist, and her latest book, IN THE GARDEN OF MONSTERS, is available in bookstores both on and offline.
session_title: Lab: AI Prompting for Marketing Excellence

name: Dakota Lowe
bio: Dakota Lowe is a social strategist with nearly a decade of experience and multiple Clios, Shortys, and Webbys recognizing her consumer-first, culture-driven campaigns. As VP of Social at Edelman, she leads social strategy for Unilever brands like Dove, Vaseline, and Hellmann’s. She’s also worked on Stanley1913, Fruit of the Loom, and major tech and pharma brands. Outside of work, Dakota writes Recreation, a (fun!) Substack about strategy, culture, and more.
session_title: Panel: Behind the Screens – How Agencies Scale Social

name: Dane Coles
bio: Dane Coles is a marketing strategist and paid advertising expert dedicated to forging genuine human connections between brands and their audiences. While he excels at driving business growth, his commitment extends far beyond the professional sphere. He finds his greatest inspiration in his family and in the great outdoors, particularly when climbing the magnificent peaks of Utah.
session_title: Rethinking Lead Gen: Your Blueprint for AI Growth

name: Daniel Lee
bio: Daniel Lee is a business leader with a background in product management and sales, known for creating customer-centric solutions that drive retention and long-term value. He most recently served as Product Manager for Business Prime and was previously Head of Startups at Amazon Business, where he launched and scaled a new startup segment. Passionate about solving real-world problems, Daniel thrives in diverse, collaborative environments and is committed to learning, leadership, and shared success.
session_title: Supercharge Demand Gen With Quality Leads on TikTok

name: Daniel Murray
bio: Daniel is the founder of The Marketing Millennials and Authority B2B, an agency that helps founders, leaders, and companies turn attention into authority on LinkedIn.  He is a former D1 athlete. GO BEARCATS. He works with his genius wife, Ari, who is arguably a way better marketer than he is. He’s spent most of his marketing career focused on Marketing Ops. BUT he has a lot of love for all types of marketing because to be great at Marketing Ops - you need to be well-versed in all of marketing.
session_title: 5 Tactical Ways To Grow Organically on LinkedIn

name: Daniel Zarick
bio: Daniel is the CEO and cofounder of Arrows, a sales room and customer onboarding platform for teams that run on HubSpot. Arrows is also funded by HubSpot Ventures. Previously Daniel was the second product manager at Twilio and ran a software design studio.
session_title: DEBATE: AI or Human: Ultimate Customer Retention Showdown

name: Danisha Lomax
bio: Danisha Lomax is Chief Connected Community Officer at Digitas, where she builds programs rooted in authenticity, cultural impact, and advocacy for women of color. With 10+ years in media, she leads social teams delivering holistic, culture-first activations. Her award-winning work has earned honors from AAF, Campaign US, and Cynopsis. A sought-after speaker and thought leader, Danisha’s insights appear in outlets like The Drum, Adweek, Ad Age, and Digiday.
session_title: Panel: Behind the Screens – How Agencies Scale Social

name: Dantley Davis
bio: Dantley Davis is SVP of Design at ServiceNow, where he’s focused on building intuitive, AI-powered enterprise experiences. With 20+ years in design leadership, he’s held key roles at Nike, Twitter, Meta, and Netflix. At Nike, he led digital product design across commerce and fitness apps. As Twitter’s CDO, he expanded and evolved the design org. At Netflix, he shaped streaming UX for TV and mobile. Dantley is known for building inclusive teams and using design to drive business growth. He holds a B.S. and MBA from the University of San Francisco.
session_title: How Top CMOs Are Using AI To Build B2B Creativity and Impact

name: David Hunt
bio: David Hunt is the VP of Customer Support & Solutions at HubSpot. David has worked at HubSpot for over 13 years and has helped scale the support and services teams that help thousands of customers daily. In addition to leading technical teams all around the world, he has helped oversee the development and integration of AI-powered experiences into how customers get help from HubSpot.

name: David Inman
bio:
David Inman is a sales-focused executive and entrepreneur with a proven track record of scaling teams and revenue. Previously, he led a 70+ person sales organization through 300%+ YoY growth, earning Inc. 5000 recognition three times and contributing to their 2023 exit.

As a founding partner at SalesAi, David helped position the company as a leader in AI voice for sales and CS teams. Now serving as VP of Partnerships, he leads strategic alliances and reseller growth. His background includes U.S. Air Force service, multiple tech ventures, and national recognition as a Sandler Sales Trainer.
session_title: Beyond Automation: Building a High-Trust AI Sales Org

name: Deren Rehr-Davis
bio: Deren is the SVP of Sales at JustCall and SaaS Labs, where he leads global revenue growth for one of the industry’s leading CCaaS and communication platforms. With 15+ years of experience selling transformational technology solutions to SMB, mid-market, and enterprise customers at companies including Sisense, Looker, Google, and JustCall, Deren has built and led high-performing teams of AEs, SDRs, Sales Ops, SEs, and Customer Success professionals. A results-driven leader, he has been directly responsible for generating over $500M in revenue over the past decade.
session_title: Tools for Growth: No Missed Calls With 24/7 AI Voice Agents

name: Dominique Crenn
company: Atelier Crenn, Bar Crenn, and Golden Poppy
company_normalized: atelier crenn, bar crenn, and golden poppy
bio: Dominique Crenn, Time's 2024 Most Influential honoree, is a groundbreaking French chef from Brittany. As co-owner of the three-Michelin-starred Atelier Crenn in San Francisco, she made history in 2018 as America's first female chef to earn this distinction. Her journey progressed from Jakarta's first female executive chef to earning her initial Michelin star at Luce (2009). Beyond her flagship, she operates Petit Crenn, Bar Crenn, and Paris' Golden Poppy. This James Beard winner and World's 50 Best honoree champions sustainability, innovation, and gender equality in culinary arts.
session_title: The Sustainable Scale: Growth Lessons From Dominique Crenn

name: Dr. Mark Hoffman
bio: Dr. Mark Hoffman leads collaborative intelligence at Asana's Work Innovation Lab. He transforms Asana's powerful data into actionable research and insights, helping customers assess how they work and identify opportunities for growth. Prior to joining Asana, Mark was a professor of sociology and business at Stanford University, where he developed novel computational tools for studying collaboration and social interaction.
session_title: Maximize AI-Driven Campaign Impact With Asana and HubSpot

name: Dr. Maya Shankar
bio: Maya Shankar is a cognitive scientist and creator of the podcast A Slight Change of Plans, previously named “Best Show of the Year” by Apple. She served as a Senior Policy Advisor in the Obama White House where she founded and chaired the Social and Behavioral Sciences Team. She was also appointed as the first Behavioral Science Advisor to the United Nations. Maya has a B.A. from Yale and a Ph.D. from Oxford as a Rhodes Scholar, and completed a postdoctoral fellowship in cognitive neuroscience at Stanford.
session_title: The Art of Yes, And: Amy Poehler's Improv-Driven Success

name: Dwarkesh Patel
bio: Dwarkesh Patel is the host of Dwarkesh Podcast, where he produces deeply researched interviews with both obscure intellectuals and the most influential figures of our time. Dwarkesh is also the author of “The Scaling Era: An Oral History of AI, 2019–2025,” and in 2024, Time Magazine named him one of the top 100 Most Influential People in AI.
session_title: The Future of AI: When Will We See an Intelligence Explosion

name: Dylan Sellberg
bio: Dylan is the Director of Product for HubSpot AI, leading the strategy and development of HubSpot’s AI platform and agent experiences. Over nearly a decade at HubSpot, he’s worked across major product areas; from Facebook Messenger and CRM to Calling and, most recently, Breeze. He’s helped shape the evolution of HubSpot into a complete front-office suite, now powering over 1 million customers and recognized as the world’s most loved CRM.
session_title: Breeze Agents: Your New Digital Teammates

name: Dylan Wickliffe
bio: Dylan Wickliffe is VP of Growth at media junction®, an Elite HubSpot Solutions Partner. A former HubSpotter turned Growth Strategist, he helps SaaS companies master subscription growth by aligning systems, data, and teams around scalable, customer-centric GTM strategies. Dylan has led hundreds of high-impact digital transformations for fast-growing orgs—from scrappy startups to global giants.
session_title: Meetup: Revenue Leaders Mastering Subscription Growth

name: EJ Cho
bio: EJ Cho is the Co-founder and CEO at Tofu, an AI-first marketing platform that helps B2B marketing teams execute integrated campaigns effectively and do more with less. Building off experiences at Google, Meta and Affirm, EJ has led the team at Tofu to drive revenue for GTM teams across large public enterprises to fast growing startups.
session_title: Signal to Revenue: Personalized Campaigns That Convert

name: Edwina Dendler
bio: Edwina helps B2B scale-ups build marketing strategies that drive growth, and the teams that bring them to life. She brings over 15 years of experience leading marketing and transformation across SaaS, Adtech, Fintech, TravelTech, and e-commerce, both as an in-house CMO and as an advisor. She’s currently shaping the future of AI-powered marketing at Prismic. Previously, she led the Direct-to-Consumer business unit at Pernod Ricard as CMO and served as Chief of Staff to the CEO at Criteo. Edwina is passionate about building strong teams, developing marketing leaders and learning every day.
session_title: Meetup: AI Fails and Lessons You Won't Hear Elsewhere

name: Elfried Samba
bio: Elfried Samba is Co-Founder and CEO of Butterfly Effect, the agency brands call for real community and cultural relevance. As Global Head of Social Content at Gymshark, he scaled their fitness community from 1.5M to 20M+ followers, contributing to their £1B valuation. Butterfly Effect's breakout year included partnerships with Netflix, Bumble, McDonald's, WeTransfer, Meta and Square. On LinkedIn, Elfried has generated 500M+ impressions, 9M+ engagements, and 360K+ followers, ranking as the second most engaged creator globally.
session_title: Creator CEO Playbook: Solopreneur to Media Corp in 2025

name: Elias Torres
bio: Elias Torres is founder and CEO of Agency, an AI agent for customer success teams backed by Sequoia and HubSpot Ventures. Previously, he co-founded Drift, creating the Conversational Marketing category before its $1B+ acquisition by Vista Equity. His career includes leadership roles at HubSpot, Performable, Lookery, and IBM. The Nicaraguan-born entrepreneur hosts "The American Dream with Elias Torres" podcast, supporting Latino and immigrant entrepreneurship. A USF and Harvard graduate, Elias lives in Massachusetts with his wife, three children, and dog Baloo.
session_title: Smaller Teams, Bigger Impact: CS in the Age of AI

name: Elizabeth Ronco
bio: As Senior Vice President of Product, Elizabeth Ronco oversees the vision, strategy, and execution for Madison Logic’s platform and solutions. With a strong focus on driving innovation through AI and machine learning, she leads product development, design, and global strategy initiatives, ensuring alignment with business objectives. Passionate about creating impactful, AI-powered solutions, Elizabeth integrates design, engineering, and customer insights to deliver cutting-edge products that help businesses accelerate pipeline velocity and turn insights into action faster.
session_title: AI-Powered ABM: Revolutionize Your Marketing Strategy

name: Elle Grossenbacher
bio: Elle Grossenbacher is a product marketing leader and host of the Product Marketing Adventures podcast, where she highlights world-class PMMs and their wins. Over the past decade, she has led GTM strategies, launches, and partner programs for Fortune 100s and high-growth tech brands, driving results like 300%+ pipeline growth. She has held product marketing roles at Cisco, Twilio, and Gartner, and holds an MBA and dual BAs in Design and Psychology from UC Davis.
session_title: DEBATE: Why Founder-Led Brands Falter

name: Emily Davidson
bio: Emily Davidson is the Enterprise Marketing Director of Sandler, with 8+ years of experience working with a diverse portfolio of brands. She has spent the last four years building the Enterprise Marketing strategy from the ground up with her team, and previously worked on global marketing campaigns with consumer brands such as John Deere, NBCUniversal, and PepsiCo. She loves to get in the trenches with new AI and technology platforms, viewing them as the key for building reliable and sustainable marketing systems of the future.
session_title: Transforming Buyer Intent Signals Into Revenue With HubSpot

name: Emily He
bio: Emily He is a seasoned Silicon Valley executive and transformative marketing leader with over two decades of experience at the forefront of innovation in enterprise technology. She is the Chief Marketing Officer at Gong and leads brand, demand, and integrated marketing strategy. Her career spans leadership positions across product, marketing, and sales functions in categories like Customer Experience (CX), Human Capital Management (HCM), ERP, and Supply Chain. She brings a rare blend of strategic vision, storytelling acumen, and operational excellence to everything she does.
session_title: Sound Bites: Bite-Sized Takes on the Future of AI in GTM

name: Emma Lynch
bio: Emma Lynch is the Co-Founder and Managing Director of BBD Boom, one of the UK’s fastest-growing HubSpot Elite Partners. With a sharp focus on sustainable partner growth and operational excellence, Emma has scaled Boom from a two-person start-up into a powerhouse consultancy delivering high-impact HubSpot solutions for B2B businesses across tech, real estate, the event industry, travel and more. Her expertise lies in building high-performing teams, creating scalable service models, and driving profitability without compromising culture.

name: Emma Pitts
bio: Emma is the founder and CEO of PullSpark, an award-winning creative agency known for innovative content, strategy and brand experiences. She launched the producer-led agency after identifying a niche in live events and now partners with brands like The Home Depot, Chick-fil-A, Delta and more. Emma gives back through pro bono video work and board service with the Atlanta Children’s Shelter. Named to EY’s Entrepreneurial Winning Women in 2024, Emma has led PullSpark to its third Inc. 5000 win and repeat honors from Atlanta Business Chronicle.
session_title: The TwoEleven Matrix™: Solving the Marketing Memory Problem

name: Emma Stratton
bio: Emma Stratton is a messaging expert and founder of Punchy, a training and consulting firm that helps tech companies win on messaging. Since 2016, Emma has developed messaging for 175+ companies like Loom, Outreach, and Uber, and has trained 1,600+ marketers how to write messaging that resonates, including teams at Salesforce, Microsoft, Atlassian, Zoom, and Oracle. She is the author of the best-selling book Make It Punchy: How to Write Simple Tech Messaging That Wins Hearts, Minds and Markets. Emma lives in Portland, Oregon with her husband and three kids.
session_title: How to Write Better Messaging for Your AI Product

name: Enrique Hoyos
bio: Enrique Hoyos is CMO at Pexels (part of Canva), where he leads marketing, product, and monetization. Through a blend of data analysis and human insight, he’s helped scale to 45M+ monthly sessions, grow the content library to more than 3M photos and videos, while strengthening integration with Canva’s 225M+ monthly users.  A curious problem solver at heart, Enrique breaks down complex business challenges into actionable strategies that drive value across the entire user journey.
session_title: Beyond Acquisition: Turning Users Into Lifelong Customers

name: Eric M. Bailey
bio: Eric M. Bailey is the bestselling author and president of Bailey Strategic Innovation Group, one of the fastest-growing human communication consulting firms in the United States. Eric has a unique set of life experiences that includes earning a Master’s Degree in Leadership and Organizational Development from Saint Louis University, helping NFL Pro-Bowler Larry Fitzgerald pet a rhinoceros, doing barrel rolls in an F-16, and chatting with LL Cool J on the campus of Harvard University. Eric believes that no matter what life throws at you theres either a lesson to be learned or a story to be told.
session_title: Using Brain Science To Explain Irrational Behavior

name: Erika Deal
bio: Erika Deal is a Senior Product Manager at HubSpot, leading sales reporting experiences that help sales leaders turn data into action. With over a decade in tech—including roles building sales solutions at Outreach and Redfin—she’s passionate about building tools that enable sales teams to work smarter and drive results.
session_title: What’s New for Sellers With HubSpot

name: Erika Fisher
bio: Erika Fisher is the Chief Legal Officer at HubSpot, where she leads global legal and compliance functions and helps shape responsible, secure AI innovation. She previously served as Chief Administrative and Legal Officer at Atlassian, overseeing Legal, People, and Customer Support. A tech industry veteran with over 15 years of experience, Erika began her legal career at Weil, Gotshal & Manges. She holds a J.D. from the University of Pennsylvania and a B.A. from the University of Miami. She also serves on the board of the Business Software Alliance.
session_title: AI-Powered Growth: Move Fast Without Losing Control

name: Erin Reddick
bio: Erin Reddick is a visionary leader and the founder of ChatBlackGPT, a pioneering AI chatbot dedicated to providing insights and perspectives rooted in Black culture, history, and experiences. Erin's work emphasizes cultural awareness, sensitivity, and respect, making her a sought-after speaker on topics related to the African diaspora, racial equality, and technology's role in promoting inclusivity.
session_title: Centering Culture in the Future of AI

name: Eshu Kalra
bio: Eshu Kalra is the product leader behind Wyzard.ai, an AI-native Marketing Copilot. He focuses on turning deep tech like LLMs and prompt engineering into real business impact. As AVP of Product, he leads the strategy and execution of sophisticated AI agents that transform how SaaS companies qualify and convert leads. With 13+ years in tech and 18 product launches across four industries, Eshu brings deep expertise in AI innovation, GTM strategy, and building scalable MarTech platforms with measurable, global impact.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Unify Data, Speed Up Funnel With AI Agents

name: Evan Hamilton
bio: Evan Hamilton has been building community professional for nearly 20 years. While today he is Director of Community at HubSpot, for 5 years Evan led community at Reddit during a period of massive growth and change. Evan focuses on retaining the human nature of community while ensuring community programs are delivering growth, retention, and dollar value.  Evan was a 2024 TOP100 Influencer & Strategist in Customer Marketing and Advocacy from Base and in 2023 Evan’s work won both Community-Led Company of the Year from GTM Alliance and Most Innovative Companies in Social Media from Forbes.
session_title: What Comes After Content Marketing

name: Evan Lauterborn
bio: Evan Lauterborn leads marketing for The Hustle, and as spent his career connecting founders, builders and aspiring entrepreneurs with the resources they need.
session_title: Meetup: The Hustle—for Founders and Future Founders

name: Eyal Orgil
bio: Eyal has over 30 years of experience leading sales teams, crafting marketing strategies, and guiding product management. As a co-founder of DealHub, he is an expert in quoting and contract management solutions. Leveraging his deep understanding of market trends and customer needs, Eyal helps organizations deploy agile revenue solutions. As Chief Product Officer, he leads DealHub’s AI vision and roadmap, driving innovation and shaping the future of intelligent sales.
session_title: GTM Acceleration With AI: From Quote To Revenue

name: Felicia Macias
bio: Felicia Macias is the innovative Marketing Technology Manager at Yamaha, with 10+ years of experience driving digital innovation and technology growth. She began her career in FinTech, transforming fragmented systems into scalable tech stacks. At Yamaha, she leads complex MarTech initiatives and delivers impactful, future-ready solutions. Passionate about tech and strategy, Felicia pushes the boundaries of what's possible. Outside of work, she enjoys time with her family, pets, and tending to her vibrant reef aquarium.
session_title: Enterprise Marketing Principles for Personalized Experiences

name: Francis Ndicu
bio: Francis is a Senior Product Manager on HubSpot's Omni-channel team. He's worked at HubSpot for 9+ years.
session_title: What’s New for Sellers With HubSpot

name: Gabriela Gironas
bio: Gabriela Gironas (they/them) is a Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree and a leading voice at the intersection of music, tech, and the creator economy. As Senior Director of Creator Strategy at Wasserman, they partner with brands like Taco Bell and Google Play to navigate the evolving creator and social landscape. A child of Bolivian immigrants, they’ve spent over a decade championing underrepresented voices through roles at YouTube, Reddit, and Fender, and are the founder of the creative consulting agency Cool Shit, Cool People.
session_title: Panel: Behind the Screens – How Agencies Scale Social

name: Gabrielle Herrera
bio: Gabrielle Herrera is an award-winning customer marketer & community manager at HubSpot, overseeing the HubSpot Community Champions and INBOUND Correspondents programs. Known for her ability to scale customer initiatives, she harnesses the passion of the Community Champions to deliver trusted peer perspectives, addressing seismic shifts in SEO strategy while positioning advocates as experts and industry thought leaders.
session_title: Meetup: Community Champions and HUG Leaders

name: Genever Oppong
bio: Genever Oppong is a private equity investor helping companies scale through capital and expertise. With investment banking experience at BlackRock and Goldman Sachs, she closed over $10B in transactions. At Goldman, she supported the "Council For Advancement of Racial Equity." She founded Akoma Wellness and co-founded Black Women Investors Network, empowering Black women investors while providing capital to diverse founders. She serves on the associate board of America Needs You, supporting first-generation college students with career development and mentorship.
session_title: How to Get Your Business Venture Capital Ready

name: George B. Thomas
bio: George B. Thomas owns Sidekick Strategies, a HubSpot Partner Agency, where he helps businesses grow using the right HubSpot tools. Beyond HubSpot, George inspires growth, equipping individuals with tools, strategies, and mindsets to flourish. As the creator and host of the HubHeroes podcast, he shares insights on HubSpot, inbound marketing, and leadership. When not working, George is a speaker, emcee, husband, father, and friend, dedicated to lifelong learning and human-centric success, aligned with his Superhuman Framework—rooted in love, purpose, passion, and persistence.
session_title: The Secret to SuperHuman-Centric Marketing That Converts

name: George Davis
bio: George Davis is Director of Product for HubSpot's AI Platform team.  Prior to its acquisition by HubSpot earlier this year, he served co-founder and CEO at Frame AI, a VC-backed AI platform that connected unstructured data to enterprise business processes.  George's career has included building AI systems for automated trading, education, bioinformatics, and defense applications.  He holds a PhD in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University.
session_title: Winning at Both Growth and Trust in the AI Age

name: George Munguia
bio: George Munguia is the CEO and co-founder of Harmony Intelligence Inc., backed by the founders of HubSpot, Typeform, and Apollo. Harmony helps GTM teams unify sales and marketing data into a single source of truth, transforming it into insights, actions, and metrics that drive pipeline and revenue. George was previously the co-founder of Coconut VA, which was bootstrapped to $3M ARR in under three years.
session_title: The Next Big AIdea

name: Glennon Doyle
bio: Glennon Doyle is the CEO and Founder of Treat Media, creating art "for humans who want to stay human." A #1 New York Times bestselling author of Untamed, Love Warrior, and Carry On, Warrior. Named among the "50 Most Powerful People in Podcasting," she co-hosts the chart-topping "We Can Do Hard Things" podcast with over 500 million plays. Doyle executive produced the Sundance award-winning film "Come See Me in the Good Light" and founded Together Rising, which distributed $55M+ to those in crisis. Her latest book with Abby Wambach and Amanda Doyle is hailed as "the guidebook for being alive."
session_title: Hard Things, Strong People: Glennon Doyle on Resilience

name: Graham Collins
bio: Graham is the Head of Partnerships at QuotaPath, where he spearheads strategic alliances with HubSpot and a wide network of tech companies as well as Solutions Partners in the HubSpot ecosystem. Prior to that, he led sales and marketing teams at several SaaS companies. He’s passionate about sales compensation, craft beer, woodworking, and petting dogs.
session_title: HubSpot Developer Platform: Showcase and AMA

name: Graham Hawkins
bio: Graham Hawkins is a globally recognised speaker, author and B2B sales thought leader.   Recently voted (by Crunchbase) in the World’s Top 25 Sales Leaders, Graham is an Amazon International Best-Selling Author, and a LinkedIn Top Voice (2018) award winner with a substantial social media following.  A serial entrepreneur, Graham founded SalesTribe in 2017, and today, Graham is Co-Founder & CEO of the world’s first AI-Guided Selling company called Qoos.ai.    A previous HubSpot Ambassador, Graham helps businesses transform to meet the demands of the Customer-Led era.
session_title: Deep Selling - A New Sales Mindset, Skill-Set and Tool-Set

name: Guy Yalif
bio: Guy Yalif is a B2B marketing executive with over 20 years of experience helping marketers use AI to drive revenue on websites and across digital advertising. Currently Webflow's Chief Evangelist, Guy has been a marketing leader at Twitter, BrightRoll, Tradeweave, Yahoo, and Intellimize. He has been a part of four successful exits and was cofounder and CEO of Intellimize, which Webflow acquired in 2024. He was also an aerospace engineer and made the oh-so-typical journey from there to CMO.
session_title: AEO for the CMO: An Actionable Maturity Model

name: Hannah Ajikawo
bio: An INBOUND24 Speaker, Hannah is the CEO & Founder of Revenue Funnel. Bringing 17 years o f GTM experience to the table, Hannah describes herself as a mum, GTM obsessive, and diversity advocate. Hannah is passionate about giving salespeople the best opportunities for success. Day to day she works with global tech scale-ups, creates authentic GTM content online & shares the mysteries and wonders of parenthood. A Hubspot Modern Sales Leader, Linkedin Top Voice and [In]sider, Hannah is considered among the top B2B Sales Thought Leaders worldwide.
session_title: 8ish GTM AI Agent Hacks My 8yo Kid Accidentally Figured Out

name: Hannah Goldberg
bio: Hannah currently works at HubSpot as a Principal Solutions Engineer within the corporate segment. She has been with HubSpot for nine years and is based in Boston.
session_title: Master Marketing Automation With Journey Automation

name: Hannah Lee
bio: Hannah Lee is a seasoned Product Marketing Manager at Asana, helping marketing teams mobilize around clear goals, get more impact from their resources, and confidently scale any campaign workflow.
session_title: Maximize AI-Driven Campaign Impact With Asana and HubSpot

name: Helen Russell
bio: As chief people officer at HubSpot, Helen Russell has global responsibility for the company’s most important asset — its people. She leads recruiting; learning and talent management; total rewards; culture; environmental, social, and governance (ESG); diversity, inclusion, and belonging (DI&B); and people operations. Helen is passionate about nurturing a culture that accelerates business and employee growth. Seasoned in scaling organizations, she brings over 25 years of people leadership experience across multiple industries, geographies, and sizes of businesses.
session_title: The Impact of Making People Uncomfortable

name: Humberto Castaneda
bio: Humberto was born in Bogota, Colombia and moved to attend high school and then College in Washington State.  He worked at Microsoft for many years, developing video games, and then joined Google in 2014.  He worked on the Google Maps Street View product, Google Meet, and most recently is focused on 3P integrations for Google Workspace.
session_title: AI Powered Sales and Marketing With Google Workspace

name: Iggy Perez
bio: The CRO at Escala24x7 holds a BA in Finance and Marketing and an MBA from East Tennessee State University. Ignacio (Iggy) has over 15 years of sales leadership experience, including teams for relevant Infor and Cisco partners in LatAm. He has been with Escala24x7 since its inception and grew the partner to the largest in Latin America and has worked with AWS teams all over Spanish-speaking LatAm for the past 7 years.
session_title: Drive Predictable Revenue With a Process-First Approach

name: Isaac Min
bio: Isaac Min is a Senior Product Marketing Manager on HubSpot's Marketing Hub team. He specializes in bringing products to market that showcase the transformative power of marketing automation and personalization.
session_title: Master Marketing Automation With Journey Automation

name: Isaac Takushi
bio: Isaac Takushi (he/him) is Manager of Ecosystem Quality on HubSpot’s Developer Relations team. Since joining HubSpot in 2016, he has supported developers and app partners through roles in Customer Support, Developer Support, and App Certification. Isaac now leads a team that drives quality programs across the HubSpot Marketplace. He is passionate about helping partners build trusted, high-impact solutions on HubSpot’s platform. Isaac lives in Olympia, WA with his wife and senior cat, Charles. In his spare time, Isaac enjoys hiking, birding, and photography."

name: Izzy Kim
bio: Izzy leads Clay Cohorts, a peer-to-peer learning initiative at Clay. She graduated from the University of Pennsylvania where she studied Computer Science and Entrepreneurship. Prior to Clay, she worked as a Product Manager at Samsara. At Clay, she's been creating fun "unhinged" education content that gets everyone excited to learn GTM engineering.
session_title: The Creative Process — Iterating Towards GTM Alpha

name: Jacqui Malis Becker
bio: Jacqui Malis Becker is a Senior Product Manager at HubSpot, now leading the marketplace platform strategy to unify product experiences and introduce new offerings. Previously, she drove social media product strategy within Marketing Hub for 5 years, bringing expertise in content creation and audience engagement from a background in Advertising Production. She is passionate about leveraging AI to streamline how users discover and connect with the tools they needs to get their jobs done faster within the HubSpot ecosystem.
session_title: Unifying Your Tech Stack: HubSpot's Marketplace Reimagined

name: Jahaira Fajardo
bio: Angélica Medina (she/her/ella) and Jahaira Fajardo (she’s/they) are the Co-Founders of Inlakech dance Academy, based in Oakland, CA. They founded the first Queer & Trans Salsa and Bachata dance school in the states. They have been building spaces of healing through dance since 2017. They are the first Queer couple with a female lead to win the World Latin Dance Cup....TWICE!
session_title: Meetup: The Spot Bachata Class

name: Jaime Diglio
bio: CEO of The WIN Room™, Jaime Diglio is a TEDx speaker, author of Moneyball Leadership, and Entrepreneurship Sales Coach at Harvard. Her WIN Room Programs help sellers perform their best when it matters most by combining emotional + social intelligence for a competitive edge in an AI world. A former Microsoft leader and Gartner Top 1% seller, she coaches leaders and teams to master the new ROI: Return on Interactions™ - because the real win today is measuring and mastering moments that matter.
session_title: Founder-Led Sales: How to Win Paying Customers

name: Jake Blocker
bio: Jake is a marketing director who knows that good marketing isn’t about shouting the loudest—it’s about saying the right thing at the right time. With a background spanning digital strategy, content, and making complex ideas actually make sense, he’s helped brands cut through the noise and connect in a way that feels human. At INBOUND, he’ll be breaking down the IA tool Open-Narrow-Close, showing how marketers can craft messages that engage (without making people hit “unsubscribe”). Expect straight talk, actionable insights, and maybe a pop culture reference or two.
session_title: Meetup: Small (But Mighty) Marketing Teams

name: James Buckley
bio: James Buckley is the host of the Sell Better Daily Sales Show, where he has appeared on over 250 episodes, sharing actionable insights to help sales professionals level up their outreach and conversion strategies. Known as ”SayWhatSales”, James brings an energetic and engaging approach to sales education, making complex concepts easy to apply. With a deep passion for helping salespeople become ”difficult to ignore” through persistence, professionalism, and personality, James is a sought-after speaker and industry thought leader dedicated to elevating the sales community.
session_title: Panel: The Guide to Effective Multi-Channel Cold Outreach

name: Jamie Elgie
bio: Jamie Elgie is a seasoned marketing executive with deep digital expertise and classical CPG training. Formerly CMO at Wilson Electronics and SVP/COO at Navico, he now leads CoDev Market, specializing in sales development strategy. Jamie's insights on product launches, digital execution, and cross-functional alignment have been featured in Forbes and LinkedIn.
session_title: Drive Predictable Revenue With a Process-First Approach

name: Jan Bogaert
bio: Jan Bogaert is a Senior Product Marketing Manager at HubSpot, based in Ghent, Belgium. He joined HubSpot back in 2019 through the PieSync acquisition and has since driven go-to-market strategies for Operations Hub and now Sales Hub. Jan blends a linguistics background with a passion for storytelling to craft compelling product narratives.
session_title: Sales Data Revolution: Unlocking Revenue Intelligence

name: Janelle Apaydin
bio: Janelle Apaydin is Head of Marketing for HubSpot for Startups, a global program helping founders scale smarter with the right tools, resources, and connections. Previously, she led ecosystem marketing at VC firm 500 Global, supporting programs and partnerships worldwide. A former founder and current startup mentor, Janelle is passionate about helping early-stage companies grow. She holds a BA in Communications with a Business minor from Santa Clara University.
session_title: Why Agents Won't Survive Your Fragmented Stack

name: Jared Williams
bio: Jared Williams is the EVP of Engineering at HubSpot, where he leads engineering teams and technical strategy. Since joining in 2011, he has held several leadership roles. He was a founding engineer of HubSpot’s CRM, led efforts to scale the customer data platform and infrastructure, and helped acquire and integrate PieSync. He played a key role in launching Operations Hub, where he continues to lead platform growth.
session_title: Inside Smart CRM: How Unified Data Unlocks Growth

name: Jason Seeba
bio: Jason Seeba is CMO at OneSignal, helping brands power smarter messaging at scale. A Silicon Valley marketing leader known for building and scaling startups, he’s led marketing at seven companies – including unicorns Bloomreach and Eightfold. Along with being an early adopter of new technology, he advises AI startups like Clari, OfferFit, and Leadspace. Jason holds a BA and MBA from Azusa Pacific University.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Add Push and SMS to HubSpot With OneSignal

name: Jay Nathan
bio: Jay a tech executive and entrepreneur passionate about growing businesses, building high-performing teams, and delivering value for customers. Over the past 20+ years he's built and led post-sales customer success, services, support and account management teams across a range of companies—from bootstrapped startups to publicly traded enterprises, $1M to $600M in ARR. His expertise lies in driving customer retention, expansion, and channel-led growth.
session_title: How We Decreased Customer Churn by 28%

name: Jay Schwedelson
company: GURU Media Hub, SubjectLine.com, Outcome Media
company_normalized: guru media hub, subjectline.com, outcome media
bio: Jay Schwedelson is one of the top marketing experts in the U.S.   Jay is Founder of SubjectLine.com, the leading free subject-line rating tool, ranked top 1% of all websites worldwide.   Jay is the Founder of GURU Media Hub, which hosts the GURU conference, the world’s largest email marketing event, as well as other major marketing events that attract 50,000 attendees annually.  His podcast, “Do This, Not That!: For Marketers,” has been ranked #1 out of 50,000 marketing podcasts in U.S.  His agency, Outcome Media, executes 40,000 marketing campaigns annually for many of the world’s most iconic brands.
session_title: Best Practices Are Garbage: In Marketing and Life

name: Jayde Powell
bio:
Jayde I. Powell is a creatorpreneur and social media strategist from Atlanta, Georgia. With over 12 years of experience in marketing, Jayde has taken her knowledge and established herself as a well-respected creative and voice in both the advertising industry and the creator economy.

She is the founder and head of creative at The Em Dash Co., a multidisciplinary social-first creative agency specializing in strategy, creative direction, and content creation. There, she has led projects for global brands such as Netflix, Timberland, LinkedIn, The Coca-Cola Company, and more.
session_title: Panel: Behind the Screens – How Agencies Scale Social

name: Jean Kang
bio: #1 Project Management Influencer & LinkedIn Top Voice. Ex-tech leader at Pinterest, Figma, LinkedIn and Meta. LinkedIn Learning instructor with 200K+ learners. Featured in Business Insider, Fast Company, Forbes. Grew from 1K to 200K+ followers in 16 months.
session_title: Data Storytelling: Customer Insights for Content Strategy

name: Jean Merlain
bio: Jean Merlain is a Product Marketing Manager at Wistia, where he leads the go-to-market strategy for webinars, lead capture tools, and integrations—including the one you’ll see in action today with HubSpot. With over a decade of experience across marketing ops, demand gen, and go-to-market strategy, Jean focuses on connecting Wistia’s features to real business outcomes. In this session, he’ll show how marketers can generate more leads, automate follow-ups, and get more value from every video. When he’s not building launch plans, you’ll find him exploring new art forms, discovering fresh music, or experimenting with new AI technologies to elevate his marketing game.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Turn Video Views Into Action With Wistia

name: Jed Mahrle
bio: Jed Mahrle is the Founder of Practical Prospecting, a leading resource for outbound sales strategies. Jed is also a frequent host and guest on the Sell Better Daily Sales Show. Starting his sales career in 2019 with no experience or degree, Jed quickly became the top-performing SDR at PandaDoc, where he later scaled the outbound team from 3 to 18 reps. He now shares his proven frameworks for booking meetings and driving pipeline through his popular Practical Prospecting newsletter and live sessions, helping sales professionals refine their outbound approach and close more deals.
session_title: Panel: The Guide to Effective Multi-Channel Cold Outreach

name: Jeff Harry
bio: Jeff Harry combines positive psychology and play to heal workplaces, help teams build psychological safety and assist individuals in addressing their biggest challenges through embracing a play-oriented approach to work. Jeff was selected by BambooHR & Engagedly as one of the Top 100 HR Influencers and has been featured in the NY Times, Mashable, Upworthy, Huffpost, Shondaland, Wired, NPR, NatGeo, & Forbes. Jeff has worked with Google, Microsoft, Southwest Airlines, Adobe, the NFL, Amazon, and Facebook, helping their staff to infuse more play into the day-to-day.
session_title: Work Is Broken…Play Can Fix It!

name: Jeff Whitlock
bio: Jeff is the CEO of Grain, a role he took on after holding a few other roles at the company, including President, Head of Product and Head of Operations. Before Grain, Jeff started his career at McKinsey, then joined Vivint Smart Home. He’s held multiple product leadership roles in early stage startups, and co-founded multiple startups of his own.
session_title: DEBATE: AI or Human: Ultimate Customer Retention Showdown

name: Jehad Begraoui
bio: Jehad (or Jeje, as she likes to be called) loves to take full advantage of inbound marketing opportunities and build long-lasting relationships with customers. Originally from Germany, she is currently based in France. When she's not behind the camera, you'll find her on the dance floor - she's always in action with salsa, bachata and especially kizomba
session_title: Lab: Making Customer Success Impact Undeniable

name: Jen Spencer
bio: Jen Spencer is a strategic executive, advisor, and growth leader focused on increasing leadership representation. She has built and scaled high-performing teams, led organizations through transformative growth, and driven GTM excellence—all while keeping people and culture at the center.  As the leader of Legacy Executive Club, Jen helps women and underrepresented leaders rise in executive roles by fixing the leadership pipeline and closing the “broken rung.” She also advises SaaS companies Graphio and iCustomer.ai, guiding strategy and GTM execution.
session_title: The Leadership Gap Is Costing You—Here’s How to Fix It

name: Jennifer Li
bio: Jennifer Li is a General Partner at a16z, and is focused on enterprise and infrastructure investments in data systems, developer tools, and AI. She’s passionate about open source and loves products that abstract complex problems to a simple interface. She’s on the boards of ElevenLabs, Ideogram, Mintlify, Mux, Pylon, Rasa, and Stacker and is a board observer at dbt Labs, Fal.ai, Fivetran, Motherduck, NX, Upstash, and Vantage. Prior to joining a16z in 2018, Jennifer led product at Solvvy (acquired by Zoom) and led self-service and analytics products at AppDynamics (acquired by Cisco for $3.7B).
session_title: The AI Growth Stack: VC Insights on the New GTM Playbook

name: Jennifer Yepez-Blundell
bio:
Jennifer Yepez-Blundell is the Co-Founder of DRAFTED, a media platform that speaks sports through the lens of Latina culture. With a deep understanding of the New Majority, DRAFTED reaches Latinas through cultural content, community and commerce. 

Prior to Co-Founding DRAFTED, Jennifer spent over a decade in corporate America working at top agencies leading Multicultural & Sports Strategy. She is a former Collegiate Athlete and her vision of shifting the commercial narratives around Latinas in sports runs deep as a Mom to two-young daughters.
session_title: Center Stage: Women Changing the Game in Sports Marketing

name: Jihan Donawa
bio: With over 18 years of turning events into can’t-miss experiences, Jihan has produced everything from high-stakes conferences to the VIP-est of intimate events. Jihan navigated the wild world of tech partnerships and now serves as Sr. Growth Marketing Manager at Swoogo, where she leads event strategy, grows Swoogo’s vibrant community, and makes sure event professionals feel like the rockstars they are. You’ll find Jihan repping Swoogo at industry conferences, where she’s part strategist, part hype squad, and always overdressed.
session_title: From Humbeat to Drumbeat: Scaling the Power of Micro Events

name: Jinny Chen
bio: Jinny Chen is the co-founder of Cello, a platform giving everyone their own hyper-personal stylist. Before launching Cello, she built predictive models in EY’s data and analytics practice and later led customer success strategy at LinkedIn. Drawing on her background in data analytics, product, and nonprofit leadership, Jinny focuses on building scalable, human-centered solutions. She earned her MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business, where she met her co-founder, Caden, and is a proud UVA alum.
session_title: The Next Big AIdea

name: Joanna Buchholz
bio: Joanna is a 21-year Deloitte veteran specializing in sales and marketing planning and digital transformation. She helps clients boost performance by aligning sales, marketing, and finance through strategy, technology, and analytics. A leader in Deloitte’s Marketing Planning offering and a founding member of its Anaplan alliance, Joanna is passionate about connecting platforms across Deloitte’s ecosystem to help clients reach their goals.
session_title: Unlocking ROI: Where Marketing and Finance Align

name: Jocelyn Horn
bio:
Jocelyn Horn is a Principal Product Marketing Manager at HubSpot, where she leads the marketing strategy and vision for HubSpot’s Smart CRM. With a diverse range of experience spanning event marketing, campaign management, and product marketing roles, Jocelyn has an undeniable passion for customer storytelling and an unwavering obsession towards understanding audience needs. 

When not thinking about marketing, Jocelyn can be found in Atlanta, Georgia, tending to her patio garden, reading, binging podcasts on foot, and enjoying the company of her fourteen year old muted calico cat, Callie Mae.
session_title: Unifying Customer Relationships With AI, Data, and CRM

name: John Bissett
bio: After a light-bulb moment John made the leap from Process Engineer to SaaS/Software sales, marrying two things he has a passion for – problem-solving and behavioral psychology. In a move he’s never looked back from, he’s enjoyed a successful career as a seller, and Director of global sales teams where he took a hands-on coaching approach. After rolling out the Slingshot methodology with great success whilst at Lockheed Martin’s Airport Software business, John joined Calum and Slingshot in 2017. He’s on a crusade to help sellers elevate our profession to the noble and respected standing it rightly deserves.

name: John Heritage
bio: John Heritage is CEO of Evenbound, a HubSpot partner agency helping industrial companies across North America and the UK grow through end-to-end solutions on HubSpot. Since 2010, he’s led digital transformation and marketing strategy initiatives that drive measurable growth. With a passion for practical innovation, John brings real-world insight into how industrial businesses can adopt AI and automation to stay competitive.
session_title: Meetup: Improving Customer Experience in Manufacturing

name: John Mothershead
bio: John Mothershead is the Director of Marketing Analytics and Member Success at Youth on Course, where he fuels membership growth, optimizes automated systems, and elevates engagement across digital platforms. With over a decade in digital marketing and operations, John harnesses data-driven insights and cutting-edge tools to drive innovation and scale impact. His expertise spans AI-powered communication, strategic partnerships, and collaborative solutions that boost organizational success.
session_title: From Beta to Breakthrough: Making Early Adoption Your DNA

name: Jon Dick
bio: Jon is the Chief Customer Success Officer at HubSpot, where he’s passionate about helping millions of organizations grow better by delivering exceptional AI powered customer experiences at scale. Before leading Customer Success, Jon built and led marketing teams at HubSpot, Trunk Club, and Klout—driving growth through brand storytelling, digital innovation, and customer-centric strategy. He earned his MBA from Harvard Business School and has a background in improvised comedy, an experience that continues to shape his leadership style.
session_title: Customer Value Revolution: 5 Shifts for CS Breakthrough

name: Jonas De Mets
bio: Jonas De Mets is a data-driven RevOps expert and co-founder of Koalify, an App that simplifies deduplication in HubSpot. Formerly a consultant for B2B SaaS scale-ups and VP of Data & Ops at Teamleader, he's passionate about clean CRM data and smart automation.
session_title: Scaling With AI: Growth Lessons From Early Founders

name: Jonathan Burg
bio: Jonathan Burg is an operational leader that designs high-performance revenue organizations and category leading brands to increase enterprise value, engage employees and achieve profitable growth on the HubSpot Platform.
session_title: Revenue Modeling 101: Plan, Predict and Scale for 2026

name: Jonathan Hunt
bio: Jonathan Hunt is the VP of Media at HubSpot and Head of The Hustle where he leads content, monetization and audience growth for HubSpot's ecosystem of video, audio, newsletter, text and creator content. Today, HubSpot Media reaches tens of millions month people each month, and converts tens of thousands of them into new demand. Previously, he led marketing and content strategy for National Geographic, VICE, Vox Media and Complex.
session_title: New Content Playbook: AI and Creative Strategies That Work

name: Jonathan Lister
bio:
Jonathan Lister is the Chief Operating Officer at Vidyard where he oversees all of Vidyard's go-to-market operations. With extensive experience in sales leadership, Jonathan has helped scale multiple billion dollar businesses.

Previously, Jonathan served as VP of Global Sales for LinkedIn Sales Solutions and VP North America Sales at LinkedIn Marketing Solutions. He has also held executive roles at Google and AOL in Europe and Canada. Jonathan earned an MBA from the Rotman School of Management and a BA from the University of Toronto.
session_title: Sound Bites: Bite-Sized Takes on the Future of AI in GTM

name: Jonathan Tablada
bio: Jonathan Tablada is a Lead Marketer at HubSpot, where he drives global paid advertising strategy across Google, Bing, Gartner, and more. He specializes in performance marketing, budget optimization, and navigating the evolving role of AI in advertising
session_title: Rethinking Lead Gen: Your Blueprint for AI Growth

name: Josh Coyne
bio: Josh Coyne joined Kleiner Perkins in 2017 and focuses on partnering with the next generation of enterprise software and financial services founders. Josh has invested in companies including Figma, Loom, Moveworks, Rippling, Stord, Synthesia, and UiPath. Prior to joining Kleiner Perkins, Josh was at Qatalyst Partners, where he helped advise on multi-billion dollar acquisitions, leveraged buyouts, and financings across a variety of technology sectors. Originally from Cleveland, Josh graduated summa cum laude from Boston College with a degree in Computer Science and Finance.
session_title: The AI Growth Stack: VC Insights on the New GTM Playbook

name: Joshua Rodriguez
bio: Joshua Rodriguez joined Inselligence in August 2023, bringing strong revenue operations expertise. Formerly Head of Growth at Safety Plus, he led outbound sales and drove 165% YoY growth, leading to acquisition. He also helped scale healthcare startups ICS and Voalte, and led 315% growth at TigerText in three years. Joshua’s background includes ops experience at Platinum Equity. He holds a BA in Business Administration from Concordia University Irvine.
session_title: Drive Predictable Revenue With a Process-First Approach

name: Juan DeAngulo
bio: Juan is Co-Founder of Inselligence, bringing deep sales, process, and organizational leadership experience. He advises the executive team on scaling from startup to sustainable enterprise. Previously, he was Co-Founder at Elion Partners, helping grow it into a $4B real estate platform. He also held leadership roles at Transwestern Investment Management. Juan holds degrees from East Tennessee State and Regent University, and is an HBS OPM alum. He serves on several boards, including Inselligence.
session_title: Drive Predictable Revenue With a Process-First Approach

name: Juanita Moreno
bio: Juanita Moreno, born in Medellín, Colombia, is a Principal Inbound Professor at HubSpot Academy. With 15 years of experience in digital marketing and instructional design, she creates educational content in Spanish to help professionals excel in the digital business world. She is passionate about education, traveling, and learning from different cultures, and also leads a personal project focused on ocean awareness and environmental sustainability.
session_title: HubSpot AI Power: Custom Solutions Driving Unlimited Growth

name: Julia Fraser
bio:
Julia Fraser is the Executive Vice President of the Americas at Sinch, the global CPaaS
(Communications Platform as a Service) platform that is pioneering the way the world
communicates through its Customer Cloud Communications. Julia is a bold, ambitious leader
with over 20 years of experience in technology and telecom. Her background in customer
success ensures that customers remain at the heart of everything Sinch does. She has a
proven track record of spearheading revenue generation strategies, forging strategic
partnerships, optimizing sales and customer success processes, and fostering high-performing
teams to drive exponential revenue growth.
Before joining Sinch, Julia served as Senior Vice President, Customer Success, North America
at Lumen Technologies. In this role, she led initiatives that significantly improved customer
satisfaction and drove substantial revenue growth. Prior to this, she was Vice President of
Sales, UK & Ireland at Lumen Technologies where she excelled in driving sales performance
and expanding market presence.
Julia’s extensive experience also includes various leadership positions at Level 3
Communications, to include Vice President of Professional Services & Specialist Sales EMEA,
Vice President of Access Management EMEA, and Senior Director of Customer Service and
Operations. Her leadership was pivotal in transforming underperforming teams into high-
achieving units and optimizing network expenses to deliver significant EBITDA improvements.
Julia holds a Bachelor of Law degree from Staffordshire University, United Kingdom.

name: Julie Zhuo
bio: After a light-bulb moment John made the leap from Process Engineer to SaaS/Software sales, marrying two things he has a passion for – problem-solving and behavioral psychology. In a move he’s never looked back from, he’s enjoyed a successful career as a seller, and Director of global sales teams where he took a hands-on coaching approach. After rolling out the Slingshot methodology with great success whilst at Lockheed Martin’s Airport Software business, John joined Calum and Slingshot in 2017. He’s on a crusade to help sellers elevate our profession to the noble and respected standing it rightly deserves.

name: Karen Ng
bio: Karen is the EVP of Product at HubSpot, where she leads product strategy and execution across product management, design, and partnerships to help millions of organizations grow better. Karen's mission is personal—she grew up watching her mom's small business fold during disruption and that experience drives her work today. In under three years, Karen has had vital impact across Smart CRM, Operations Hub, Breeze Intelligence, and HubSpot's partner ecosystem. Karen is an established product leader who builds for real people with real stakes, always pushing the boundaries of what's possible.
session_title: Inside the AI Ecosystem: How Platforms Win Together

name: Karina Martinez
bio: Karina Martinez is the Co-Founder, CEO of DRAFTED, and a 2024 HOLA Powerhouse Latina changing the commercial messaging for Latinas in sports. As a two-time founder, she brings cultural fluency and brand strategy together to reshape how industries engage Latina audiences.
session_title: Center Stage: Women Changing the Game in Sports Marketing

name: Kate Ahlering
bio: Kate Ahlering serves as the Vice President of North America for LinkedIn’s Sales Solutions business, leading the teams behind LinkedIn’s flagship product for sales professionals, Sales Navigator. With over two decades of go-to-market leadership experience, Kate has a strong track record of building and scaling high-growth businesses. Prior to LinkedIn, she served as Chief Revenue Officer at both Calendly and Glassdoor, where she led teams across sales, customer success, and revenue operations.
session_title: Sound Bites: Bite-Sized Takes on the Future of AI in GTM

name: Kate DiLeo
bio: Kate DiLeo is a brand architect, author, and the founder of The Brand Trifecta®, the proven methodology that has helped thousands of organizations craft brands that bring more prospects to the table and generate more customers who buy. Kate’s approach is rooted in the belief that brand is the path of least resistance to revenue. She teaches you to eliminate complex and ineffective storytelling by delivering a simple yet provocative message that tells prospects what you do, how you solve their problem, and how you differ from the competition. The outcome? Brand conversations that convert.
session_title: ENCORE: Teardown Live! Does Their Story Suck – Or Convert?!

name: Kate Long
bio: Kate Long is VP of Marketing at Crunchbase, where she leads global strategy, brand positioning, and adoption of its AI-powered predictive intelligence solution. She has driven growth and transformation at Assurance IQ (a $400M+ Prudential subsidiary), PitchBook Data, and Expedia Group. With expertise in scaling marketing for data and intelligence platforms, Kate is helping transform how investors and business leaders move from historical data to real-time, AI-driven insights.
session_title: From Lead to Loyalty: Is B2B Too Obsessed With the Funnel?

name: Katherine Man
