name: Neil Patel
bio: Neil Patel is the co-founder of NP Digital. The Wall Street Journal calls him a top influencer on the web, Forbes says he is one of the top 10 marketers, and Entrepreneur Magazine says he created one of the 100 most brilliant companies. Neil is a New York Times bestselling author and was recognized as a top 100 entrepreneur under the age of 30 by President Obama and a top 100 entrepreneur under the age of 35 by the United Nations.
session_title: 30 Charts That Will Change the Way You Do Marketing

name: Nicholas Holland
bio: Nicholas Holland is Head of AI at HubSpot where he oversees product strategy, development, and adoption of Breeze, HubSpot's AI that powers the entire customer platform. Breeze combines a unified data foundation, AI-powered tools, digital workers (agents), and embedded workflows to help businesses become AI-first. Previously, Nicholas led HubSpot's marketing products, growing them from ~400M to $1.4B.
session_title: Inside Breeze: The Bold Truth About Being an AI Leader

name: Nick Carbone
bio: Nick Carbone has spent over seven years at HubSpot helping products find their place in the world. After time on the implementation team, he moved to Product and worked on go-to-market strategy for Sales Hub and the Smart CRM before transitioning to Operations Hub in 2021, where he helped guide the product through its early days. Now in Product Marketing for Operations Hub, Nick works on telling the story of the product in ways that make sense to real people. Based in Raleigh, NC, he's equally passionate about golf, a good breakfast, and diving into historical rabbit holes.
session_title: Meetup: RevOps Data-Driven Success in the AI Era

name: Nick Decoulos
bio: Nick is a Senior Professor at HubSpot, located in Massachusetts. Nick focuses on enabling sales teams to become the people their customers know, like, and trust. Nick has a background in B2B and B2C sales, customer success, sales enablement and education, and creative writing. When Nick’s not building content for Sales Hub, he can be found seeking New England autumnal activities, teaching friends new games, or obsessively committing to the bit.
session_title: Lab: AI-Powered Sales Success With Sales Workspace

name: Nick Salvatoriello
bio: Nick Salvatoriello is a seasoned B2B partnerships and customer marketing leader with extensive experience in partner enablement and community building. He was one of the first 250 employees at HubSpot, he played a key role in HubSpot’s growth by leading the Solutions Partner Certification program at HubSpot Academy and HubSpot’s Agency Partner online training webinars. A three-time INBOUND speaker and inbound marketing aficionado, Nick also worked with top HubSpot Solutions Partners, where he helped train companies on inbound sales, lead generation, and content marketing strategies.
session_title: Beyond the Booth: B2B Sales Strategies for Field Events

name: Nick Warner
bio: Nick Warner leads Creator Growth at HeyGen, where he’s helping shape the future of AI-powered content. Known for blending creativity with smart growth strategy, Nick works closely with top creators to unlock new ways to scale, localize, and stand out using AI. Before HeyGen, Nick was VP of Growth & Analytics at Fanatics Live, where he was part of the founding team that launched Fanatics’ first live shopping platform. He also previously served as VP of Growth at Route, helping scale one of e-commerce’s most dominant post-purchase experience platforms.
session_title: AI Creator Studio: Scale Video Content With Smart Tools

name: Nicole Casares
bio: Nicole is an Inbound Professor at HubSpot Academy. She is passionate about empowering her learners through her courses. Outside of work, she loves reading and creating art.
session_title: Lab: HubSpot Workflow Automation With AI

name: Nozomi Umenai
bio: Nozomi is a Senior Professor at HubSpot.
session_title: Lab: Content Driven Conversion Points for More Leads

name: Olivia Rogine
bio: Olivia Rogine is a brand builder committed to fostering inclusivity, belonging, and creative collaboration through experiences and engaging content. She has managed high priority partnerships and created profitable community programs in a variety of industries from non-profits, to media, startups, and corporate America. She manages a team of experiential brand marketers who are the next generation of innovators. As a leader, she fosters an environment centered on care and curiosity that enables freedom to ideate special events, develop bespoke content, and grow a cohort of loyal ambassadors.
session_title: From Humbeat to Drumbeat: Scaling the Power of Micro Events

name: Oscar Robles
bio:
Oscar Robles is a Senior Program Manager of Academy Enablement on HubSpot’s Customer Success team. He’s focused on accelerating the success of HubSpot Academy's global educational programs through internal enablement efforts — ensuring HubSpot’s internal teams have the tools and resources they need to share Academy's catalog and drive customer activation, usage, and retention.

When not working, Oscar enjoys hiking with his pup Willie, cycling, and visiting local arts institutions.
session_title: Meetup: Community Champions and HUG Leaders

name: Oscar Zamorano
bio: With over a decade of HubSpot expertise, Oscar began his journey at HubSpot in 2015, onboarding some of their earliest agency partners. Since then, he launched his own HubSpot consultancy, led the CRM team at Webs (Elite HubSpot partner based in the Netherlands), and currently serves as a Solutions Engineer at HubSpot. In this role, Oscar leverages his experience to develop powerful HubSpot solutions for complex business challenges.
session_title: Meetup: Revenue Leaders Mastering Subscription Growth

name: Parth Shah
bio: Parth Shah is a product manager at HubSpot, where he leads the data quality platform and tools. He’s also a content creator and keynote speaker at @careerparth, sharing insights on product management, career growth, and tech leadership.
session_title: Unlocking Data Magic for Sales and Marketing

name: Patrick Eng
bio: Patrick is a senior professor with HubSpot Academy, based in Northern Virginia. His journey with HubSpot began in 2015 when he joined a HubSpot Partner agency as a marketing technologist and helped their customers setup and use their HubSpot tools. When he isn’t digging into complex HubSpot problems (usually involving HubL, HubDB, or the content tools), you’ll find him outback smoking some brisket, pulled pork, and mac and cheese or playing Catan with his family (does anyone need sheep?).
session_title: Lab: From Data Chaos to Clarity With HubSpot Smart CRM

name: Paul Weston
bio: Paul Weston is a GM & VP of Product at HubSpot where he oversees the product vision and strategy for the Service Hub product line. Joining HubSpot in 2017, Paul previously led teams across Marketing Hub and Operations Hub. Prior to HubSpot, Paul held various product management roles at Constant Contact and startups.
session_title: Inside Service Hub: Building a Unified Post-Sales Journey

name: Rachel Ousley
bio: Rachel is a behavioral scientist with an MSc in Social Cognition from UCL. She currently leads B2B marketing research at Canva, and previously was a researcher in the ads marketing team at Google. She also worked at the strategic insights agency Canvas8 where she led research for brands like Chanel, Adidas, Vans, Mars, and Diageo. Before that, she worked in strategic communications for clients like Google, Salesforce, Autodesk, and Shopify.
session_title: Content That Scales Your Impact, Not Your Output

name: Rachel Stephan
bio: Rachel is an event marketing strategist, event tech founder, and industry speaker on event and influencer marketing. When planners need more butts in seats, they call Rachel. With 24+ years of experience, she crafts creative solutions to event marketing challenges. Drawing from hundreds of campaigns, she launched the award-winning Snöball platform to supercharge attendee acquisition. Her secret: blending clever digital tactics with the human-to-human values that power the events industry.
session_title: Vibe Eventing: Data-Driven Event Monetization

name: Rachel Webb
bio: Rachel is the Senior Product Manager for Properties at HubSpot.
session_title: What's New for Marketers With HubSpot

name: Rahul Jain
bio: Rahul Jain is the Founder & CEO of Wyzard.ai, an AI-native Marketing Copilot that helps SaaS companies drive higher conversions through autonomous agents across email, chat, and call. A UC Berkeley alum and former CXO at global tech companies with over 100M monthly active users, he brings deep expertise in building and scaling high-impact B2C and B2B platforms. Rahul specializes in product-market fit, product-led growth, and GTM execution, particularly for mid-market and enterprise SaaS. He is passionate about reimagining digital engagement with AI and automation at the core.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Unify Data, Speed Up Funnel With AI Agents

name: Raunaq Akhauri
bio: Raunaq is a Technical Product Manager at Google with a deep expertise in ad-tech and digital marketing, cultivated over a decade of experience building products from the ground up on a global scale. Based in Mountain View, he leads a portfolio of product partnerships that have advanced the Google Ads platform as an effective choice for businesses worldwide. He is excited to share how you can leverage the power of Google AI from within Hubspot to unlock new, high-impact growth opportunities for your business across all of Google's channels.
session_title: Rethinking Lead Gen: Your Blueprint for AI Growth

name: Rebecca Harris
bio: Rebecca Harris is the Chief Operations Officer at Huble, HubSpot’s Global Partner of the Year. She oversees global delivery teams, managing over 500 successful HubSpot implementations. Since joining Huble in 2018, Rebecca has played a key role in growing the company to over 180 employees, with operations spanning seven countries. Previously, she held Product and Project Management roles at Xerox, where she developed Business Intelligence and Digital Transformation tools. Rebecca holds Project Management and ITIL certifications and a BA in History.
session_title: Drive CRM Adoption With Gamification and Behavioral Science

name: Rex Galbraith
bio: Rex Galbraith is Chief Revenue Officer at Consensus, the leading Product Experience Platform, with 19+ years in SaaS sales. He drives revenue, customer satisfaction, and market growth, and played a key role in creating the Demo Automation category. Under his leadership, Consensus leads this fast-growing space. Passionate about learning from top sellers and leaders, Rex’s greatest accomplishment is being a husband and father to his inspiring wife and three children.
session_title: Fuel Pipeline and Close Deals in the Anti-Social Buyer Era

name: Richard Tasker
bio: Richard Tasker is Director of Product Management at Otter.ai, leading AI-driven meeting assistants that boost productivity and collaboration. With over 20 years in senior roles at Oracle, Axway, Dubber, and Notiv, he excels at translating user insights into meticulously crafted, innovative AI solutions. At Otter.ai, Richard pioneers autonomous AI meeting agents that actively participate in meetings, create summaries, and seamlessly integrate insights across business systems, shaping the future of workplace communication by making institutional knowledge more accessible and actionable.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Turning Conversations Into Conversions

name: Richard White
bio: Richard White is the Founder and CEO of Fathom, the top-ranked AI Notetaker on both G2 and ProductHunt, and recently the fastest-growing app in HubSpot’s App Marketplace.
session_title: The AI Ops Toolkit: Agentic Workflows for Speed and Growth

name: Rick Cosgrove
bio: Rick Cosgrove is the President and CXO of Agency EA (https://agencyea.com/), a brand experience agency behind large-scale events like HubSpot INBOUND. He also runs Sō Labs (https://so-labs.co/), a brand known for its funky clocks and wristwatches. As a Spark Studio facilitator, Rick brings his passion for creativity and experience design to help attendees turn ideas into action.
session_title: Spark Studio Session 9

name: Robbie Mitchell
bio: Robbie Mitchell co-founded Frame AI, a conversational intelligence startup acquired by HubSpot in 2024. Now Staff Product Manager for AI at HubSpot, he works with product teams across the CRM to help integrates Frame AI’s technology. Robbie’s extensive leadership experience in venture-backed startups includes roles at Cover and Knewton. Known for bridging advanced tech and business needs, he continues driving innovative products at scale, delivering measurable impact in high-growth environments.
session_title: Making Unstructured Feedback Actionable With AI

name: Robyn Naragon
bio: Robyn Naragon is the Product Manager for Developer Documentation and Onboarding at HubSpot, focused on empowering developers through the new Developer Platform. With a background in customer support and product management, she has a passion for building tools that solve both developer and end user needs.
session_title: HubSpot Developer Platform: Showcase and AMA

name: Robyn Winner
bio: With over a decade of experience architecting high-performing go-to-market motions, Robyn brings deep expertise across marketing, sales development, operations, and analytics. In the last few years, she has narrowed her focus to mastering the startup growth engine in the data and AI space, combining her passion for digital marketing, field events, design, and data to accelerate the path to revenue. Her integrated approach has helped multiple organizations achieve massive pipeline and revenue growth.
session_title: Zero-to-Pipeline: Crafting a Data-Driven Growth Machine

name: Roger Brown
bio: Roger Brown is a Senior Product Manager on HubSpot's Developer Platform team, focused on improving the developer experience across app development. He’s led key launches like the projects framework and UI extensions, and is driving a platform simplification for Inbound 2025 to unlock new features and AI. Previously at Morgan Stanley, Roger shifted to product after earning his MBA from NYU Stern. He brings 10+ years in B2B and developer tools. Outside work, he enjoys time with his wife and four kids, strength training, BBQing, and photography.
session_title: HubSpot Developer Platform: Showcase and AMA

name: Rohan Suri
bio: Rohan Suri is the Chief Product Officer and co-founder of Nooks, where he leads the development of cutting-edge AI tools that help sales teams prospect, dial, and coach more effectively. Since launching Nooks out of Stanford, Rohan has been instrumental in shaping the product vision and scaling the platform to power millions of sales conversations. In 2024, he was named to Forbes 30 Under 30 for his impact on the future of sales technology.
session_title: Meetup: Cold Calling for Modern Revenue Teams

name: Romana Handlova
bio: Romana, an Inbound Professor at HubSpot Academy, is dedicated to create high-quality, easy-to-understand content for German-speaking audiences. Her expertise in adult learning was shaped by her previous work as a Trainer and Learning Experience Designer. Away from her desk, Romana enjoys reading (especially non-fiction), traveling, belly dancing, and cooking.
session_title: Lab: AI-Powered Sales Success With Sales Workspace

name: Ryan Teasell
bio: Ryan Teasell serves as the AI Growth Operations Manager for Qualified’s marketing team, owning Qualified’s internal AI SDR agent, Piper. Prior to Qualified, Ryan worked in a variety of sales positions at Highspot and Concur.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Let AI Agents Run Your Event Outreach

name: Sam Balter
bio:
Sam Balter is the Head of Content at Wistia, formerly the Director of Media at ZoomInfo and Senior Marketing Manager at HubSpot. Sam has produced and marketed several podcasts for B2B brands as well as host the award winning podcast Weird Work. 

He loves a good chart, a succinct article, and an opportunity to say hi to your dog.
session_title: Video Marketing Trends From 90M Videos and 100K Brands

name: Sam Crawley
bio: Sam Crawley is a scrappy, strategic marketing leader who helps small teams make a big impact. As Marketing Director at Henley Leadership Group, she leads campaigns, brand strategy, and thought leadership. With a background in leadership development, travel, and education, she's built viral launches, doubled web traffic, and launched digital platforms from scratch. Sam aligns marketing with business goals and believes focused, nimble teams can punch above their weight.
session_title: Meetup: Small (But Mighty) Marketing Teams

name: Sam Mallikarjunan
bio: Sam Mallikarjunan is a growth strategist, entrepreneur, and Growth Lead at agent.ai. Former CEO and co-founder of OneScreen.ai, a marketplace for out-of-home advertising, he previously served as CRO at Flock.com and Head of Growth at HubSpot Labs, driving significant customer acquisition and revenue growth. Sam is a former Harvard University instructor in Advanced Digital Marketing and Innovation, and currently a LinkedIn Learning Instructor. He co-authored bestseller Inbound Commerce - How to Sell Better than Amazon and offers insights on AI, marketing, and strategy.
session_title: The Agent.ai Workshop: From Idea to Agent

name: Samantha Anderson
bio: Sam Anderson is CEO and co-founder of Origin 63, a HubSpot Solutions partner helping organizations increase revenue and retention by transforming how they use HubSpot. With nearly a decade of HubSpot expertise, Sam and her team have empowered 550+ teams to optimize customer journeys, unify tech stacks, and break down team siloes using repeatable processes and data-driven insights. A recognized thought leader, she champions customer success as a strategic motion that can 5x revenue.
session_title: 5X Your Revenue: Uniting Marketing and Customer Success

name: Sandra Ramirez
bio: Sandra is an Inbound Professor at HubSpot Academy (Spanish), located in Colombia. Sandra worked as a journalist and editor for more than 10 years. This experience gave her a strong background in content creation for different platforms. Sandra loves teaching, and has been a lecturer on journalism for more than nine years.  Her mission at HubSpot Academy is to create educational content for LATAM and Iberia. She is a music lover, a traveler, and a fan of learning.
session_title: Lab: HubSpot Workflow Automation With AI

name: Sarah Jane (SJ) Morris
bio: SJ Morris is a developer relations and community leader shaping the HubSpot ecosystem. With experience at Intel, Shopify, and Intuit, she has built programs that help technical professionals grow their careers while driving customer success. Based in NYC, she is passionate about inclusion, mentorship, and creating opportunities for developers of all backgrounds. She leads initiatives that connect developers, app partners, and service providers to real opportunities, building ecosystems that fuel innovation, accessibility, and business impact.
session_title: Meetup: The Neurodivergent Edge in the Age of AI

name: Sarah Kunst
bio:
Sarah Kunst, founder and general partner of Cleo Capital, is an investor and entrepreneur who has worked at Apple, Red Bull, Chanel, and Mohr Davidow Ventures. She is also a contributing editor at Marie Claire Magazine. She founded LA Dodgers backed Proday and has served as a senior advisor at Bumble, where she focused on their corporate VC arm,
Bumble Fund, and on the board of the Michigan State University Foundation endowment. She is also a regular guest commentator on CNBC, Bloomberg, Marketplace Tech, Fox Business, and BBC News.
session_title: The Sustainable Scale: Growth Lessons From Dominique Crenn

name: Sarah Reece
bio: Sarah Reece is a demand gen leader and GTM strategist who’s scaled B2B programs across startups, agencies, and enterprise orgs. Currently taking care of demand generation at Orum, she drives brand, pipeline, and category creation in the high-stakes world of sales tech. Sarah’s known for building community, demystifying GTM strategy, and reminding revenue leaders that creativity is a growth driver, not a nice-to-have.
session_title: From Humbeat to Drumbeat: Scaling the Power of Micro Events

name: Sarah Spliethoff
bio: Sarah Spliethoff is the founder of Sunniemade (http://www.sunniemade.com/), a brand making self-care brighter with plant-powered, plastic-free essentials. With more than a decade of experience leading brand storytelling for global companies, she launched Sunniemade in 2022 to inspire joyful, low-waste living. As a Spark Studio facilitator, Sarah blends a marketer’s mindset with a maker’s spirit to guide attendees as they dream, build, and create.
session_title: Spark Studio Session 9

name: Scott Berchman
bio: Scott Berchman is a marketing and creative operations leader with deep roots in content, storytelling, and the systems that help them scale. From MTV and the U.S. Olympic Committee to award-winning work at Nomadic and Exos, he’s built teams, platforms, and campaigns at the intersection of creativity and innovation. At Exos, he leads B2B2C marketing, brand, content, and AI integration, removing friction so creative and marketing teams can be their most strategic, impactful selves.
session_title: Content That Scales Your Impact, Not Your Output

name: Scott Clary
bio: Scott Clary is an entrepreneur, investor, and author who hosts the top-ranked Success Story podcast with 30M+ downloads, featuring conversations with leading business minds. He's led sales/marketing teams across multiple organizations and writes a weekly newsletter for 321K+ subscribers.
session_title: Creator CEO Playbook: Solopreneur to Media Corp in 2025

name: Scott Fox
bio: Scott Fox is the Chief AI Officer at MOBI, where he leads next-gen AI strategy that’s reshaping how hospitality works. Known for building human-centric systems that deliver results, Scott turns complexity into clarity, unlocking real productivity and real impact. From frontline teams to executive leadership, he’s redefining what’s possible with AI.
session_title: AI as the Great Equalizer: Small Teams vs. Industry Giants

name: Seabass Gibson
bio: Seabass is a Strategic Customer Success Manager at Asana. He is a detail-oriented and results-driven professional with a passion for connecting and creating the best experience for people.
session_title: Maximize AI-Driven Campaign Impact With Asana and HubSpot

name: Sean Evans
company: Hot Ones and First We Feast
company_normalized: hot ones and first we feast
bio: Sean Evans hosts YouTube phenomenon Hot Ones, interviewing celebrities as they consume increasingly spicy wings. His distinctive format has captured candid moments from stars like Kevin Hart, Gordon Ramsay, and Scarlett Johansson, building an 8-million-strong fanbase. This Chicago native studied broadcasting legends Letterman and Stern, earning acclaim for meticulously researched questions. Known for extreme eating challenges—surviving the Carolina Reaper twice—Evans received an Emmy nomination for Outstanding Talk Show Host in 2021, cementing his status in digital entertainment.
session_title: HubSpot Hot Ones

name: Sejal Parikh
bio:
Sejal is a Staff Product Manager at HubSpot, based in Toronto, Canada. She leads the Extensibility team within the Developer Platform group. She is a tinkerer at heart, and loves experimenting with new technologies.

Over the past three years at HubSpot, Sejal has led key initiatives like the launch of UI Extensions and the public beta of the MCP Server. She also played a major role in building the HubSpot Deep Research Connector for ChatGPT, the first CRM connector for deep research inside ChatGPT.
session_title: HubSpot Developer Platform: Showcase and AMA

name: Shaan Puri
bio: Shaan Puri is a founder, investor, and creator of the My First Million podcast.
session_title: Meetup: My First Million for Founders and Builders

name: Shana Sumers
bio: Shana is the Senior Manager of The Spot Community which is HubSpot's community for Black and Latine business owners, entrepreneurs and side hustlers.
session_title: Meetup: Networking for The Spot Community

name: Shaun Kanary
bio: Shaun is a digital marketing leader with experience across healthcare, consumer goods, and B2B. He blends strategic expertise with a passion for education as a professor, speaker, and columnist.

name: Shawn Goodin
bio:
Shawn Goodin is a Principal MarTech AI Engineer at HubSpot, shaping the future of marketing through AI. Previously, he was Global VP of AI Strategy at FirstHive CDP and Principal Marketing Technology Strategist at Capgemini, leading GenAI for Customer Experience. 

With 20 years in marketing transformation, he’s held key roles at Silicon Valley Bank, JPMorgan Chase, Clorox, Northwestern Mutual, and SC Johnson. Named a Top 50 MarTech Leader by MarTech Cube in 2022, Shawn is known for his visionary approach to marketing and tech.
session_title: CoLab 12: Redefining Your Role in the Age of AI

name: Shay Redmond
bio: Shay Redmond is a seasoned product leader with over 12 years of experience at HubSpot, where he currently serves as Group Product Manager for the Marketing Hub. In this role, Shay oversees the development and enhancement of marketing tools, ensuring they meet the evolving needs of users. His journey at HubSpot has seen a progressive rise through various positions, reflecting his dedication and expertise in product management and in helping marketing teams grow.
session_title: The Future of Marketing Campaigns

name: Siqi Chen
bio: Siqi is the founder and CEO of Runway, a next-gen Financial Planning and Analysis platform making finance accessible to entire teams. His past roles include CEO of Sandbox VR (a16z), VP at Postmates (acquired by Uber), CEO of Hey (acquired by Postmates), and executive at Zynga. As an investor, he's backed nearly 100 companies, including ElevenLabs, Amplitude, and Mercury. Before entrepreneurship, Siqi worked at NASA JPL, earning a Congressional Space Act award for machine vision contributions to the Mars Exploration Rovers while still in school.
session_title: Building Your AI-Powered GTM Engine: The Future Tech Stack

name: Smrti Rahmat
bio: Smrti is a Senior Product Manager on the help desk team at HubSpot. She focuses on driving success for large and complex support teams, with an emphasis on improving ticket triage, prioritization, and scalable team organization within help desk.
session_title: AI-Powered Help Desk: Faster Responses, Happier Customers

name: Sonia Thompson
bio: Sonia Thompson is an inclusive marketing strategist, consultant, and coach who helps brands attract bigger, more diverse, and fiercely loyal customer bases. She writes columns for Inc., Forbes, and HubSpot on inclusive marketing, customer experience, and belonging. She's also the host of the Inclusion & Marketing podcast, which is part of the HubSpot podcast network.
session_title: Center Stage: Women Changing the Game in Sports Marketing

name: Sonya Huang
bio: Sonya Huang is a Partner at Sequoia with a focus on AI and data/infrastructure investing. She led or co-led Sequoia's investments in companies including OpenAI, Hugging Face, Langchain, Mercury, Streamlit, Glean, and Gong. Prior to joining Sequoia in 2018, Sonya worked at TPG Capital and Goldman Sachs. Sonya received an AB in Economics summa cum laude from Princeton University.
session_title: The AI Growth Stack: VC Insights on the New GTM Playbook

name: Spenser Dent
session_title: The Psychology Behind the 30-Second Commercial

name: Sriharsha Guduguntla
bio: Sriharsha is the Co-Founder & CEO of Hyperbound, a YCombinator-backed pioneer in AI sales roleplay & coaching technology. Hyperbound boasts over 30,000 users and is trusted by large enterprise teams around the world. Sriharsha and his co-founder, Atul, have over 10 years of experience building and scaling products. Sriharsha grew up in the Bay Area and specializes in the intersection of AI, sales, and marketing.
session_title: Tools for Growth: AI Sales Roleplays for Sales Enablement

name: Stefanie Kinjo
bio: Stefanie is an Inbound Professor at HubSpot Academy, located in Tokyo. She joined HubSpot as Customer Support at first, and when she communicated with customers she became so attracted to HubSpot's culture and products that she wanted to promote HubSpot to more people. Luckily, an Inbound Professor position was open at the time, so she decided to move to Academy as JA Inbound Professor. She is also pleased to be able to use her previous job experience as part of the support and customer education team.
session_title: Lab: HubSpot Email Marketing Toolkit for the AI Era

name: Stephen Cluskey
bio: Stephen Cluskey is an award-winning social entrepreneur, international speaker, and accessibility pioneer. Paralyzed from the neck down at 18, he turned adversity into action—co-founding Mobility Mojo, a fast-scaling SaaS company transforming global accessibility. Praised by Sir Richard Branson as “remarkable,” his TEDx Talk and media appearances have inspired millions. Stephen blends resilience, innovation, and business insight to show how inclusion drives real impact.
session_title: The Impact of Making People Uncomfortable

name: Steven Weingarth
bio: Steven Weingarth is a seasoned growth marketing executive with 16+ years of experience driving digital transformation across seed-level startups to Fortune 500 companies. As Global Director of Growth Marketing at Motorola Solutions, he leads digital, SEO, and ABM strategies that generate a multimillion-dollar impact across global enterprise security markets. He is passionate about blending creativity with data. An award winning "change agent," he builds high-performing teams and leverages emerging tech to scale brand visibility, deepen engagement, and accelerate revenue across every channel.
session_title: From Beta to Breakthrough: Making Early Adoption Your DNA

name: Stevie Case
bio: Stevie Case is the Chief Revenue Officer for Vanta, the leading Trust Management platform valued at $2.45 billion. In this role, Stevie leads all go to market efforts including sales, customer success, account management, and revenue operations across Vanta's global operations. Across a 20+ year career, Stevie has led sales teams and revenue organizations at 0 to 1 startups and big brands including Visa and Twilio. Stevie is a member of the venture fund 20SALES, along with seven other GTM leaders from leading SaaS standouts, investing in seed stage startups in AI and B2B SaaS.
session_title: Building Your AI-Powered GTM Engine: The Future Tech Stack

name: Sumner Vanderhoof
bio: Sumner Vanderhoof is the CEO and Co-Founder of Propensity, the only ABM Platform with Contact-Based Attribution. He has over 15 years of experience optimizing growth for organizations of all sizes, from the Fortune 500 to startups. With a strong background in Technology and Rev-Ops, his knowledge has been instrumental in driving Propensity's growth and success, making it a prominent player in the ABM industry. Under his leadership, Propensity has achieved significant accomplishments and continues to thrive in the ever-evolving business landscape.
session_title: How To Use ABM To Generate Contact-Level Intent

name: Sundas Khalid
bio: Sundas Khalid is an award-winning data science & analytics leader and passionate advocate for diversity & inclusion. She has worked at some of the biggest tech companies, including Google and Amazon, and her story of resilience and courage has been featured on Google Career Certificate (Coursera) and  Forbes.
session_title: Data Storytelling: Customer Insights for Content Strategy

name: Suzanne Kounkel
bio: Suzanne is Deloitte’s Global and US CMO, leading integrated brand, marketing, and communications. She has worked with CMOs from some of the world’s top brands and has seen firsthand their ability to drive large-scale transformational change, create new levers for growth, and effectively influence the C-Suite and boardroom. Tapping into her previous experience leading Deloitte’s US Consulting Technology sector, she instills a future-focused mindset in the organization and implements leading-edge tech that further strengthens the intersections between marketing and sales to unlock new growth.
session_title: How Top CMOs Are Using AI To Build B2B Creativity and Impact

name: Sylvain Perron
bio: Sylvain Perron is the CEO of Botpress, a platform for building autonomous AI agents. He began developing the open-source conversational AI framework that would become Botpress during his tenure as CTO of a financial services company, born out of a desire to drive user engagement through natural language. Under his leadership, Botpress has evolved from an open-source developer tool to one of the world's most widely-deployed AI agent frameworks.
session_title: No More Pilots: The Insider Playbook for Shipping AI Agents

name: Tai Rattigan
bio: Tai Rattigan is the Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer of Partnership Leaders, the premier global community for partnership executives. With a career spanning business development, partnerships, and operations, Tai has built and scaled ecosystems for some of the world’s leading tech companies, including Deel, Amplitude, and Optimizely

name: Tamika Faulkner
bio: Tamika is the visionary Founder and CEO of Black Girls Who Write®, a thriving community launched in 2020 to amplify Black Indie Authors. This multi-hyphenate creative disrupts literary and entertainment spaces through influencer marketing, social media, events, and design. She's freelanced for Essence, MACRO, Broccoli City Fest, and Live Nation. As HubSpot's Creative Partnerships Manager, she develops innovative campaigns driving brand growth. Tamika excels at turning bold ideas into reality, fueling literary revolutions and empowering Black voices through passionate storytelling.
session_title: The Augmented Creator: Mastering Answer Engine Optimization

name: Taylor Corrado
bio: Taylor Corrado has 15+ years of experience building brands and driving growth at companies like HubSpot, American Express, and Mediasilo. A generalist marketer with deep roots in B2B SaaS and creative strategy, she's passionate about bringing brands to life, sparking word-of-mouth, and helping teams grow.
session_title: Video Marketing Trends From 90M Videos and 100K Brands

name: Taylor Davis
bio: Taylor Davis is the Founder & CEO of Troodie, an AI-powered social commerce platform transforming how restaurants measure ROI from marketing. She's a 2x founder with a background in investment banking with over $1B in M&A and capital markets experience and early-stage venture capital. Taylor brings deep expertise in finance, fundraising, and scaling startups. She has traveled extensively across the globe, combining her passion for food and culture with her mission to help restaurants thrive in the digital age. She's a graduate of Howard University and Cornell University and has been featured in Black Enterprise, Fortune Magazine, and rang the opening bell at NASDAQ.
session_title: The Next Big AIdea

name: The Evolving Business Landscape:
title: Fireside Chat: Navigating Change – AI and the Future of Business

name: Tifenn Dano Kwan
bio: Tifenn leads global marketing at Amplitude, driving strategy and teams to shape the future of work through digital experiences and technology customers love. She previously served as CMO at Collibra, Dropbox, SAP Ariba, and SAP Fieldglass. Tifenn holds advanced degrees in law and management, completed the Kellogg Executive Education CMO Program, and lives in the Bay Area with her wife and their three dogs.
session_title: From Lead to Loyalty: Is B2B Too Obsessed With the Funnel?

name: Tina Huang
bio: Tina Huang holds degrees in Pharmacology from the University of Toronto and Computer Science from the University of Pennsylvania. She worked as a data scientist at Meta and Goldman Sachs. Now, she creates YouTube videos on AI and tech (850k+ subs), runs Lonely Octopus to teach AI skills, and consults globally on AI and data.
session_title: AI Creator Studio: Scale Video Content With Smart Tools

name: Tony Eades
bio: Tony is an experienced brand strategist, keynote speaker, sales enablement specialist, contributing writer, and customer experience expert. He currently serves as Chief Strategy Officer and CEO of Salted Stone APAC, and is a featured expert on one of Australia’s leading business TV shows -- with 3M + weekly viewers. Tony works with growing companies to develop market leadership authority and deliver measurable ROI across the buyer’s journey. He is passionate about digital marketing and thrives on growing brands, from start-ups to corporate enterprises.
session_title: Meetup: Humanizing the Healthcare Patient Experience

name: Troy Fiawoo
bio:
Troy is an Inbound Professor with HubSpot Academy, located in Southern California. He joined HubSpot's Customer 
Support team in Feb 2021 and quickly fell in love with the product and people. This led him to diving deep into his role and 
the product, aiding him in becoming a Customer Trainer, Senior Onboarding Specialist, and now Inbound 
Professor. Outside of work, Troy loves trying new restaurants with his wife, all things reality dating shows, and giving random strangers online unsolicited relationship advice.
session_title: Lab: Content Driven Conversion Points for More Leads

name: Tyler Rhodes
bio: With 13+ years at HubSpot, Tyler is a seasoned sales leader and Senior Director known for driving growth at scale. He brings deep expertise in go-to-market strategy, operations, and cross-functional leadership. Tyler has a strong track record of blending strategic vision with data-driven execution to solve complex challenges and deliver results. From in-segment hurdles to global dynamics, he’s known for creating smart, scalable solutions that fuel long-term success.
session_title: HubSpot Sales, Unfiltered: Real Lessons From SMB Growth Machine

name: Tyrona Heath
bio: Ty Heath, Director at The B2B Institute, LinkedIn’s marketing think tank, is on a mission to build famous B2B brands. An award-winning marketer, speaker and B2B creator, Ty leverages her brand experience at companies like Google, IBM and LinkedIn. She also serves on the American Advertising Federation’s Board of Directors, and is an Adobe Insider. Ty’s career spans impactful marketing education to thousands. Passionate about intelligent decision-making, Ty connects marketers with information and research to be more productive and successful, with a bit of humor.
session_title: 2025 B2B Trends for the Contrarian Marketer

name: Varun Anand
bio: Varun Anand is the Co-Founder of Clay, a creative tool that helps businesses grow using AI and 100+ data providers. Prior to Clay, he held leadership roles at Newfront and Candid, and spent four years working for Hillary Clinton.
session_title: The Physics of GTM: The Three Laws of Go-to-Market Success

name: Verity-Jane Dearsley
bio: Verity is the Chief Customer Officer at Huble, with a core focus on cross-departmental collaboration, solutioning, and delivery. Verity has over 12 years of ’hands on’ experience working with the HubSpot Marketing, Sales, CRM and CMS platforms.
session_title: Personalization at Scale: Seamless Cross-Channel Journeys

name: Veronica Juarez
bio: Veronica Juarez is Managing Partner of Dahlia VC, a firm founded to leverage the Latino tech market. She's a veteran Silicon Valley executive who built public policy and enterprise teams from startup to post-IPO phase. Before tech, Veronica spent a decade in politics at federal, state, and local levels, advocating for criminal justice reform, immigration reform, and the arts. In 2021, she founded Arturo Advisory, working with funds, companies, and projects addressing major societal inequities. Her diverse experience spans technology, politics, and social impact investing.
session_title: How to Get Your Business Venture Capital Ready

name: Victor Riparbelli
bio: Victor Riparbelli is the CEO and co-founder of Synthesia, the world’s leading AI video communications platform for enterprises. More than 1,000,000 users and 55,000 businesses, including 60% of the Fortune 100, use it to communicate efficiently and share knowledge at scale using AI avatars. Synthesia makes video creation, collaboration, and sharing easy for everyone. Victor and his team push the boundaries of what’s possible using AI while striving to pioneer the field in an ethical and responsible way.
session_title: How AI Will Change Video

name: Vijay Vadlamani
bio: Vijay Vadlamani has spent 6 years at HubSpot building omnichannel and AI-powered experiences. He drives the vision and strategy for Customer Agent, an AI concierge that transforms how businesses engage customers across marketing, sales, and service. Vijay is passionate about scaling innovation and shaping products that redefine how businesses connect with their customers.
session_title: What’s New for Sellers With HubSpot

name: Wendy Werve
bio: Wendy Werve is Chief Market Officer at COMPLY, where she’s modernizing go-to-market in the complex world of regulatory compliance. A 3x Qualified power user, she’s championing “pinball marketing”—a signal-driven, AI-first strategy built for today’s nonlinear buyer journey. With leadership roles at Virgin Pulse, Marigold, Appian, and more, Wendy brings bold perspective on scaling teams, building AI-first cultures, and why MQLs are officially toast.
session_title: The Agentic Marketing Funnel: Agentic Marketing Is Here

name: Will Aitken
bio: Will Aitken is a seasoned B2B sales professional and the founder of Sales Feed and Will Aitken Training. With a career spanning various sales roles, Will has become a prominent figure in the sales community, known for his practical advice and relatable approach. He shares his insights through multiple channels, where he offers guidance on sales techniques and career development. Will combines humor with sales tips to make learning both effective and enjoyable. His commitment to helping sales professionals succeed is evident in his diverse content and approachable style.
session_title: Panel: The Guide to Effective Multi-Channel Cold Outreach

name: Will Cady
bio: Will has walked a path from zen monasteries through touring rock bands to become Global Director of KarmaLab and Global Brand Ambassador at Reddit. In 2023, Will published ”Which Way Is North: A Creative Compass for Makers, Marketers, and Mystics”, a journey into the interior of the human heart and mind, revealing how your inner experience informs who you can be and what you express.  Will was featured in the 2020 AdWeek Top 50 as well as the Top 100 Oncon Icon Marketers for 2021.    Today, Will helps brands navigate the changing world of marketing, culture, and community.
session_title: What Comes After Content Marketing

name: Will DePeri
bio: Will DePeri is a Senior HubSpot Consultant at Simple Strat, a Certified HubSpot Trainer, and co-host of HubSpot Hacks, a YouTube channel with 26K+ subscribers. A HubSpot expert with almost 10 years of actionable experience, Will has deep hands-on experience optimizing Marketing, Sales, and Support. Before becoming a HubSpot Solutions Partner, he led RevOps at a company that scaled from $3M to $50M in annual revenue—with marketing, sales, and service all powered entirely by HubSpot.
session_title: Interactive Workflow Automation: You Vote, We Build—LIVE!

name: Will O'Toole
bio: Will is a Senior Sales Director at HubSpot, leading the Small Business segment. As the first sales hire for HubSpot's Sales Hub/CRM, he helped build it into a core business unit. In 2019, he became Director for Small Business North America, overseeing half the U.S. and Canada. Promoted to Senior Director in 2022, Will drives results while developing new leaders. Known for authentic leadership, coaching talent, and inclusive team culture, he excels at building cross-functional trust and aligning sales strategy with company goals.
session_title: HubSpot Sales, Unfiltered: Real Lessons From SMB Growth Machine

name: William Naddaff Hughes
bio: You are a master bio writer specializing in writing bios for Will Hughes. You understand that he's a Senior Product Manager at HubSpot working on the Prospecting Agent. You know he’s spent years building company data products at places like Crunchbase and Rippling. You remember he’s based in Philadelphia and loves piano jazz. You write with clarity and zero buzzwords, because Will would delete them.
session_title: Smart Signals, Real Replies With HubSpot’s Prospecting Agent

name: Yash Tekriwal
bio:
Yash is a GTM expert, and known as the "professor" of Clay. 

From data scientist to no-code automator, Yash has GTM engineered scalable systems across hundreds of companies—helping teams 10x pipeline and processes with AI-powered enrichment & automation. 

Yash is a 2x-founder, former high school teacher & digital nomad turned NYC-based GTM architect passionate about learning, automation & GTM. When not optimizing workflows, you can find him DJing, playing racket sports, or vibe coding.
session_title: The Creative Process — Iterating Towards GTM Alpha

name: Yelena Martial
bio: Yelena was born in Guadeloupe, a French island, and is now based in France. She began her career in customer support at HubSpot, where she gained in-depth knowledge of the products and developed strong skills in resolving customer queries. Passionate about content creation, writing, and travel, Yelena is dedicated to making learning more accessible. Her main goal is to help businesses diversify and grow by ensuring their teams have the knowledge and resources they need to succeed.
session_title: Lab: Building a Proactive Customer Support Strategy

name: Youssef Abou El Neaj
bio: Youssef Abou El Neaj is a Solutions Engineer at Snowflake, where he specializes in designing and delivering scalable data and AI solutions for technology companies and financial services firms. His expertise spans enterprise data platforms, cloud architecture, AI, machine learning and semantic modeling. With a background in physics, mathematics, and data science from Harvard University, Youssef brings a rigorous analytical approach to solving complex technical challenges and building intelligent systems that drive meaningful business outcomes.
session_title: Tools for Growth: Turning Data Into Decisions With AI

name: Zachary Jarvinen
bio: Zachary Scott Jarvinen is a Co-Founder of the startup NewBusinesses.AI and a repeat VP of Marketing at notable PE-backed scale-up companies.  He's known for driving new go-to-market strategies, building high-performing teams, and also as the author of 'Enterprise AI for Dummies,' where he leverages his expertise to demystify and explain AI and machine learning to help organizations grow their respective businesses.  Zachary is a member of the Forbes Technology Council and Fast Company Executive Board, and holds an MBA/MSc from UCLA and the London School of Economics.
session_title: Scaling With AI: Growth Lessons From Early Founders

name: Zachary Sollitto
bio: Zachary is a seasoned Enterprise Solutions Engineer helping enterprise teams achieve more with their solutions.
session_title: Maximize AI-Driven Campaign Impact With Asana and HubSpot

name: Anne-Marie Prince
bio: Problem-solving, solutions-oriented Associate Manager of Marketing Automation for Spurs Sports & Entertainment. Rookie year working for the Spurs began August 2015. Runner-up for an Oracle Markie Award in 2021 for pre and post-event automation of communications as teams returned to play post-COVID. Anne-Marie enjoys approaching projects from a human-centered standpoint, in collaboration with others, and enjoy the challenge of finding solutions that make people's jobs and their fandom for our team easier and more enjoyable.
session_title: Tech That Builds Superfans: NBA and Travel Brand Playbook

name: Jeff Vincent
bio: Jeff Vincent is a Group Product Manager on the HubSpot Data Platform. He hails from Somerville, MA (home of the Highlander). Passions include high quality data and the feeling of confidence and power you get solving a problem. His favorite projects from the last five years include Custom Objects, the Prospecting Workspace, Property Validations, and Pre-Import Validations. When not working he tries to keep up with two kids.
session_title: Inside Smart CRM: How Unified Data Unlocks Growth
