name: Adam Cuzzort
bio: Adam Cuzzort is GM and VP of Product of Data Hub at HubSpot, leading the teams behind HubSpot's data foundation. His teams are focused on making trustworthy, unified data the connective tissue of HubSpot's Agentic Customer Platform, so businesses get smarter automation, agents, and insights. A 20-plus year product and engineering leader, Adam has spent his career scaling high-performing teams and helping enterprises deploy and secure AI in regulated, privacy-constrained environments.
session_title: Building the Data Foundation for Agentic AI

name: Allison Bryant
bio: Allison Bryant is a revenue-driving lifecycle leader with 12-plus years scaling B2SMB SaaS and B2C apps. Right now, she's helping beauty and wellness businesses succeed at GlossGenius, the best business software for salons and spas, where she drove double-digit lifts in subscription and upsell conversion through rigorous experimentation. Before that, she helped people start a meditation habit at Happier Meditation and raised millions of dollars for colleges and environmental nonprofits, but her proudest accomplishment is teaching her cat to “speak” via programmable buttons.
session_title: Rank Your Marketing Experiments Like a Growth Team

name: Amiria MacKinnon
bio:
Amiria MacKinnon is head of integrated marketing, APAC at HubSpot, where she leads integrated & field marketing initiatives across Australia, Asia & India. With nearly 15 years’ experience across B2B, B2C and non-profit industries, her speciality is connecting brand and strategic narratives to integrated campaigns that build mindshare and grow revenue.

She’s currently obsessing over new, creative ways to engage audiences, campaign narratives, and piloting a new hybrid team model to scale her team’s impact.
session_title: Redesigning Your Revenue Engine for Scale

name: Anastasiia Kovtunenko
bio: Anastasiia Kovtunenko is an account executive at HubSpot who is passionate about her work and helping customers succeed.
session_title: How Multi-Brand Companies Scale with HubSpot's MaM

name: Anoosh Roozrock
bio: Anoosh Roozrock is the founder and CEO of Aloware, an AI-powered contact center platform built to integrate with leading CRMs. With a Ph.D. in Astrophysics from UC Riverside and a background in software engineering, he's spent the last eight years building telephony infrastructure that connects sales and support teams with customers across voice, SMS, and AI. Anoosh has spoken at Twilio SIGNAL and regularly advises SMB teams navigating the shift from traditional dialers to AI-driven customer conversations.
session_title: The AI Handoff: When Should AI Stop and Humans Step In?

name: Arun Manoharan
bio: Arun Manoharan is a senior director of product at HubSpot.
session_title: How Data Agent Fuels Your Growth Engine With Context and Signals

name: Beeri Amiel
bio: Beeri Amiel is a director of product management at HubSpot.
session_title: Why Your Brand Isn’t Showing up and How To Win Back Demand

name: Ben Donahower
bio: Ben Donahower is CEO of No Bounds Digital, a Diamond HubSpot Agency Solutions Partner, and a three-time HubSpot Technical Expertise Award winner (2023–2025). With over a decade of hands-on experience across HubSpot's full platform, Ben specializes in pushing the limits of what's possible with automation, custom code, and AI-powered workflows. An award-winning public speaker and Toastmaster, he's known for making complex HubSpot topics approachable and immediately actionable—turning technical depth into practical wins audiences can deploy the same day.
session_title: 7 HubSpot Workflow Upgrades You Can Implement Today

name: Brigitte Gabrus
bio: Brigitte Gabrus is the director of product for Sales Hub at HubSpot.
session_title: Turning Unstructured Conversation Data into Revenue

name: Britney Cole
bio: Britney Cole is chief innovation officer at Blanchard, a premier leadership development company, where she drives AI strategy and future-of-work innovation. As an early enterprise adopter of generative AI, she helps marketing, sales, and revenue leaders build AI-augmented teams that outperform without sacrificing culture. She is a sought-after speaker on leadership evolution in the age of AI.
session_title: Turn AI Experiments Into Measurable Revenue

name: Bryan Saftler
bio: Bryan is an entrepreneurial marketer with deep experience leading product marketing, digital strategy, and global GTM for B2B and B2C teams. He's built marketing infrastructure and led strategic initiatives within billion-dollar businesses, including Databricks, Azure, Xbox, and Bing. Over 20+ years, Bryan has launched companies, advised founders, and established products in challenging categories, earning recognition from Webby, FWA, Awwwards, and SXSW Interactive. In the world of AI, he builds high-performing teams from the ground up and knows how to scale both humans and dollars.
session_title: Marketer's Modern Data Stack: How We Built PLG Automation

name: Byung Chung
bio: Byung Chung is a product lead at HubSpot, where he leads the team building HubSpot’s integration management and connected apps experience. He focuses on helping customers understand how their apps work together—and how to manage them with confidence. With a background in integrations and APIs, he enjoys making complex systems feel simple and connected. He’s especially interested in how AI can simplify integration management and support smarter decisions.
session_title: Turn HubSpot Into a System of Record Across Your Stack

name: Champ Bennett
bio:
Champ Bennett is co-founder and CEO of Capsule, a collaborative video platform helping enterprise teams create videos at scale.

A software engineer, designer, and musician by background, Champ is a three-time startup founder with over a decade of experience building tools that empower people to create and communicate. He believes AI should enhance human creativity, not replace it—and that's exactly what Capsule does.

The company has raised $19 million and works with leading B2B brands like HubSpot, Instacart, and ServiceNow to help their organizations become video-first.
session_title: HubSpot’s Playbook for Scaling Video Across the Org

name: Chris Grant
bio: A five-time HubSpot Impact Award winner and Academy Super Admin Bootcamp instructor, Chris combines 20 years of sales experience with a decade of Elite-level HubSpot implementation and consulting. Having led over 50 implementations across the full CRM Suite, Chris specializes in solving complex business process problems and turning neglected portals into powerful assets. He is a HubSpot Certified Trainer who thrives on data flow optimization and driving a culture of learning. When the laptop closes, he’s a cook, a baker, a martial arts enthusiast, and a dad.
session_title: The Decision Engine: How to Build a CRM That Drives Action

name: Christine  Lee
bio: Christine Lee is a senior inbound professor at HubSpot, where she helps professionals develop the skills to grow their careers and businesses. With a master's in clinical mental health counseling, Christine brings a unique lens to leadership development and coaching. She's also a practicing therapist, blending behavioral science with practical business education. Originally from Hong Kong and now based in New Hampshire, Christine is passionate about unlocking potential in others.
session_title: Running Loops: A Marketer's Guide to AI-Era Growth

name: Cyndi Knapic
bio: Cyndi Knapic is a lead product marketer for the HubSpot Marketplace, where she drives awareness and adoption of apps that extend what’s possible with HubSpot. She focuses on ecosystem strategy—helping businesses build smarter, more connected tech stacks. Based in New York City, she spends her time outside of work dancing, ice skating, and enthusiastically cheering from the sidelines at her son’s basketball games.
session_title: Turn HubSpot Into a System of Record Across Your Stack

name: Dan Stillgoe
bio:
Dan Stillgoe is marketing director at Blend, where he leads the company’s marketing strategy and performance. Alongside running demand for Blend itself, he plays a key role in testing and validating new approaches to growth, turning internal experimentation into proven frameworks that shape how Blend supports its clients.

Focused on pipeline impact, buyer behaviour, and conversion performance, Dan brings a practical, commercially grounded perspective to modern B2B marketing. He co-hosts the HubSpot Podcast Network show Demand Decoded.
session_title: The Loop Unplugged: Inside Modern B2B Growth

name: Darren Smith
bio: Darren is a marketing operations and systems leader with more than 15 years of experience helping high-growth companies scale smarter. Having worked across Personio, Sage, DocuSign, and Databricks, he has built and scaled marketing operations teams, developed more than 50 internal tools, and translated complex marketing needs into scalable architecture. Starting his career in digital marketing before specializing in operations, Darren brings a unique perspective on how marketers can move from execution to system design.
session_title: From Marketing Executor to Marketing Architect

name: David Meerman Scott
bio: David Meerman Scott is a marketing strategist, advisor, and bestselling author who studies how organizations grow by building passionate fan communities. His Wall Street Journal bestseller "Fanocracy" explores the power of fandom, and his book "The New Rules of Marketing and PR" has sold more than 500,000 copies in 29 languages. David serves on the HubSpot advisory board and delivers keynotes and masterclasses around the world. He loves to surf and dance the Lindy Hop.
session_title: The Fandom Playbook: Create Passionate Fans to Grow Business

name: Dax Miller
bio: Dax Miller is the founder and chief care officer of hapily, a CRM-centric events platform building HubSpot-native tools that help organizations run smarter, more connected events inside their CRM. Before hapily, Dax was a multi-time SaaS founder and product leader with a background in branding, technology, and agency work. He co-founded appchemist, where he built HubSpot apps designed to close product gaps and drive deeper CRM adoption. A builder at heart, he blends creativity with technical execution, helping teams rethink how their CRM can power meaningful experiences and growth.
session_title: Feeling Known Beats Being Targeted

name: Devin Littlefield
bio: Devin Littlefield is the owner and CEO of Market Vantage, a B2B growth agency serving software and technology companies. With over a decade of experience in digital advertising, demand generation, and revenue optimization, he helps long sales-cycle businesses build scalable, data-driven growth engines. After joining the firm in 2018 and acquiring it in 2024, Devin now leads strategy, performance alignment, and RevOps-driven programs for high-growth B2B teams.
session_title: Account-Based RevOps: Rebuilding Your Revenue Engine in 2026

name: Diana Zalaquett
bio: Diana Zalaquett is the Enablement Lead on HubSpot's Global Events team, leading enablement strategy for INBOUND and other marquee experiences. She sits at the intersection of events, RevOps, and revenue strategy, building the systems that connect registrations, internal enablement, and onsite engagement to measurable pipeline and closed-won revenue. Her work turns large-scale events into accountable growth engines grounded in real data and cross-functional alignment.
session_title: Beyond Ticket Sales: How To Prove Event ROI

name: Dr. Carmen Simon
bio: Dr. Carmen Simon is a cognitive neuroscientist, Chief Science Officer at Corporate Visions, and founder of Enhancive, an agency that helps customers create memorable communication based on how the brain works. She's the author of Impossible to Ignore and Made You Look, two widely read books on attention and memory.  Dr. Simon also teaches several courses at Stanford University on how to create messages on memory and decision science. After all, what’s the use of memory if people do nothing with it?
session_title: What Neuroscience Reveals About Sales Success

name: Elaine Zelby
bio: Elaine is the co-founder and CRO of Tofu, an AI platform that helps scale and automate go-to-market for B2B marketing teams. Prior to Tofu, she was a partner at SignalFire, an early-stage VC firm where she invested at the intersection of AI and SaaS. Before investing she built and led marketing teams at early and growth-stage startups like Slack and ConsenSys. Elaine has two small humans and spends as much time as she can outdoors trail running, cycling, hiking, or doing other active endeavors.
session_title: From Prompting to Building: Create Your First AI Agent

name: Grant Lee
bio: Grant Lee is the CEO and co-founder of Gamma, the AI-powered presentation and website design tool that has scaled to 70 million customers and $100M in ARR with only 50 employees. A Stanford-trained engineer with a background in finance and operations, Grant spent much of his early career creating countless slide decks, a pain point that inspired him to found Gamma and reimagine how people share ideas. Before Gamma, he served as COO of ClearBrain (acquired by Amplitude) and CFO of Optimizely.
session_title: The 0 to $100M ARR Playbook: Gamma’s Growth Journey

name: Hannah Fisher
bio: Hannah is a Principal Consultant at a UK Elite HubSpot Agency, bridging the gap between technical architecture and GTM strategy. Having worked in more than 100 HubSpot platforms, she specializes in transforming static CRMs into decision engines that drive strategic growth. A HubSpot Community Champion, HubSpot Certified Trainer, two-time INBOUND Correspondent, and contributor to Kyle Jepson's Tips and Tricks, Hannah designs integrated, multi-hub systems for complex global firms, turning technical builds into measurable business outcomes.
session_title: The Decision Engine: How to Build a CRM That Drives Action

name: Jaime Jurenek
bio: Jaime Jurenek is a senior product marketing Manager at HubSpot, where she drives the go-to-market strategy and vision for HubSpot Smart CRM. With a diverse background spanning marketing, customer success, account management, and journalism, she has a passion for storytelling, educating, and helping customers grow. Offline, you’ll find Jaime hiking metro beautiful Atlanta’s trails, going to art fairs with her husband, and working on her novel.
session_title: Get Your Data AI-Ready: Configure a CRM That AI Can Use

name: Jake Goldstein
bio: Jake Goldstein is director of revenue operations at Kiln, a lifestyle coworking brand, where he leads revenue strategy and systems that drive sustainable growth. With over 8 years in the industry, he has worked across community teams, sales, and revenue operations, giving him a cross-functional lens on scaling teams and markets. Based in Boston, Jake blends data, automation, and a human-centered approach to build high-performing teams and exceptional member experiences.
session_title: How Our AI BDR Increased More Human Conversations

name: Jarrod Webb
bio: Jarrod Webb is the founder and CEO of Blinq, a leading digital business card platform and system of record for in-person relationships. Founded in 2017, Blinq powers digital business cards, event lead capture, and AI-powered conversation memory for millions of professionals across more than 500,000 companies, helping turn first meetings into opportunity-rich relationships. Jarrod has been recognized on the Forbes 30 Under 30 list for consumer technology.
session_title: The Rise of the In-Person GTM Engine

name: Jay Schwedelson
bio: Jay Schwedelson has built and led businesses generating more than $400 million in revenue—by doing the one thing most marketers won’t: breaking the “best practices” everyone else blindly follows. Jay is the founder of SubjectLine.com, the world’s leading free subject line rating tool. As CEO of GURU Media Hub, he leads a powerhouse team that executes 40,000+ marketing campaigns each year for some of the world’s most iconic brands and produces the largest virtual marketing events on the planet, attracting 50,000+ attendees annually. Jay also hosts the podcast “Do This, Not That: For Marketers.
session_title: The Next 5 Years Will Not Reward the Smartest People

name: Jazmine Moore
bio: Jazmine Moore is the founder and CEO of NoirLogic, a go-to-market strategy and operations consultancy for B2B tech companies. With over a decade of experience operationalizing GTM teams, she helps companies align their people, processes, and systems before creating their architecture. Her work focuses on cutting through operational chaos so GTM teams can execute with clarity and measure what's working.
session_title: Lead Management Mastery: From Chaos to Conversion in HubSpot

name: Jen Spencer
bio: Jen Spencer is a global business executive and author of "Lead Anyway: How to Build the Career You Want When the Rules Weren't Written for You." With 20+ years leading global teams, she has scaled companies from zero to millions in revenue and navigated acquisitions by multibillion-dollar companies. Her unconventional path—from English teacher to SaaS sales and marketing leader to business executive—informs her leadership philosophy grounded in authenticity and cross-domain thinking. A 10-year INBOUND speaker, Jen's practical frameworks help professionals build authority on their own terms.
session_title: From Rep to Revenue Leader: The Confidence Gap

name: Jess Cook
bio:
Jess is a creative director turned content marketer and strategist with more than 20 years of experience conceptualizing and executing breakthrough work for B2C brands like Eggo, Rice Krispies, Blue Cross, Cottonelle, and McDonald’s, and B2B brands like Fastly and Island.

She has a knack for leading cross-functional teams toward a common goal, bringing order to the oft-chaotic creative process, crafting insight-driven global campaigns, and bringing stories to life via long- and short-form content.
session_title: How I Turned a CEO Into a Thought Leader

name: Josh Curcio
bio: Josh Curcio is the CRO and partner at protocol 80, helping technical B2B companies, especially manufacturers and industrial firms, modernize marketing, sales, and CRM without overwhelming the teams who have to live in it. With 20+ years of experience, he is a HubSpot Community Champion, Manufacturing HUG Leader (East Coast), and HubSpot Certified Trainer. Josh is an 8x FABTECH speaker, 2x INBOUND speaker, and 2x Partner Day speaker. Outside of work, he cycles, runs, and plays pick-up basketball as a proudly undersized guard.
session_title: Fix CRM Adoption: A Blueprint for Complex Sales Teams

name: Josie Lickteig
bio: Josie Lickteig is a data analyst at HubSpot, where she specializes in measurement and reporting for global events. She works at the intersection of events, demand management, and revenue marketing, designing dashboards and KPI frameworks that connect registrations, sales engagement, and pipeline impact across regions. Her work helps ground event strategy in standardized reporting, data quality, and cross-functional alignment.
session_title: Beyond Ticket Sales: How To Prove Event ROI

name: Karen McFarlane
bio: Karen McFarlane is a strategic growth advisor and Fractional CMO who helps founders and CEOs scale their businesses by building marketing functions and developing leaders. With 15 years’ experience, she has worked with startups, SMEs, and global firms to drive growth. She is an NACD-certified board director and chairs Compensation & Benefits at Hudson Valley Credit Union. Karen also co-hosts The E Word podcast on leadership and power.
session_title: Diagnose Team Tension Before It Derails Results

name: Kina Lara
bio: Kina Lara is a senior product marketer at HubSpot, where she turns complex product capabilities into clear, customer-first stories. With broad experience in B2B SaaS and go-to-market strategy, Kina focuses on helping teams understand, adopt, and get real value from HubSpot’s AI products.
session_title: The Breeze Playbook: Use Cases That Drive Real Results

name: Kolin Koehl
bio: Kolin is GM and senior director of product for Breeze Assistant and Mobile at HubSpot. Before HubSpot, he spent years building AI and voice products at Observe.AI, VoiceBase, and Medallia, shipping agent systems that automated millions of customer interactions. Now he's bringing that experience to Breeze Assistant, helping customers get instant answers, take action faster, and get more done anytime, anywhere.
session_title: Connecting Support and Success to Drive Growth

name: Lori Richardson
bio: Lori is a sales strategist who helps small business leaders grow revenue and shorten sales cycles. She started in the tech sector working for IBM and Apple and has held sales leadership roles across distribution, technology, and financial services. Lori incorporates AI agents into sales teams to drive new efficiencies and reminds leaders to focus on the human skills that make selling effective, not just efficient. A leader in inclusion, she produced The Sales Decades project - content and in-person events.
session_title: From Rep to Revenue Leader: The Confidence Gap

name: Lucy Rogers
bio: Lucy Rogers is a senior product manager on the fintech team at HubSpot, where she focuses on usage-based monetization and the systems that power how HubSpot prices, packages, and bills its products. Before HubSpot, Lucy worked at ExxonMobil, where she gained experience in large-scale operations and analytical problem-solving. Lucy is passionate about the evolving intersection of pricing and AI, and in treating pricing as a product experience that aligns customer value with business growth.
session_title: Your Pricing Model Is a Customer Experience—Make It a Good One

name: Maarten Bovend'aerde
bio: Maarten Bovend'aerde leads marketing solutions and strategy at Digital Reach, a HubSpot Diamond Partner, and Nonprofit Tech Shop. Working across SaaS, nonprofits, scale-ups, and enterprise tech means seeing RevOps problems in every form, which helps build systems based on what actually works across industries, not just what theory promises.
session_title: RevOps for Real: Systems Built for Imperfection

name: Maha Abouelenein
bio:
Maha Abouelenein is the CEO and Founder of Digital and Savvy, a global communications consulting firm with offices in the United States and the United Arab Emirates. A best-selling author, entrepreneur, and strategic communications expert, she has spent more than 30 years advising Fortune 500 companies, governments, high-profile executives, and global brands on storytelling, reputation, and personal branding.

Maha is a leading voice on storytelling in the age of AI and speaks around the world on how individuals and organizations can build trust, influence, and cultural relevance.
session_title: Build Trust Through Storytelling in a Skeptical World

name: Mahyar Raissi
bio: Mahyar Raissi is the co-founder and CEO of Quo (formerly OpenPhone), an AI-first communication platform that helps every team deliver fast, thoughtful, and personal experiences at scale. Under his leadership, Quo serves 100,000+ customers and has been rated the No. 1 business phone system for customer satisfaction on G2.
session_title: OpenPhone to Quo: How to Rebrand 7 Years In

name: Maitri Sheth
bio: Maitri is a senior product marketer at HubSpot, where she leads go-to-market strategy for AI products. She specializes in turning technically complex products into clear market narratives. With experience spanning APAC and EMEA markets, she's had a long career building GTM programs that connect product innovation directly to revenue impact.
session_title: How Data Agent Fuels Your Growth Engine With Context and Signals

name: Marcel Santilli
bio: Marcel is co-founder and CEO of GrowthX AI, which he bootstrapped from $0 to $7 million+ in months, scaled to $13 million+ profitably, and raised a $12 million Series A led by Madrona. He runs AEO and GEO for companies like Webflow, Ramp, and Lovable, and has built a 50-plus person AI content operation. Marcel launched CheckThat and the AI-Led Growth Masterclass, and has spoken at Webflow Workshop, AUTONOMOUS 2025, and Growth Cafe. He's a former CMO at Deepgram with more than 15 years in marketing and GTM at IBM, HPE, HashiCorp.
session_title: What 10,000 Hours of AEO Taught Us

name: Mark Deacon
bio: Mark Deacon is the chief revenue operations officer at Canibuild, where he leads go-to-market strategy and revenue transformation for a high-growth global SaaS company. A growth-focused operator, Mark has scaled teams and revenue through strategic AI adoption across marketing, sales, and support, delivering measurable results at scale. He also hosts The GTM Playbook Podcast, helping founders and revenue leaders navigate modern go-to-market challenges and opportunities.
session_title: Redesigning Your Revenue Engine for Scale

name: Megan Dumas
bio: Megan Dumas is a senior product marketer at HubSpot. She specializes in connecting new features to customer value, partnering with product, sales, and customer teams on positioning, release marketing, and ongoing messaging.
session_title: What’s New for Marketers

name: Meghan Parsons
bio: Meghan is a senior marketing technical specialist at HubSpot, where she's spent the last seven years and the last four-plus on the Marketing Technology team. Her day-to-day lives at the intersection of marketing strategy and technical execution--translating ambiguous asks from campaign teams into clean specs for engineers, data teams, and AI tools. She thinks in objects, schemas, and workflows, but also speaks fluent marketer. When she's not untangling automation logic, she's probably ranking new restaurants on Beli or cuddling her tiny dog, Ollie.
session_title: Translating Ideas into Buildable Systems

name: Michael Barnett
bio: Michael Barnett is a social marketing entrepreneur and CEO of InGo, focused on turning event attendees into advocates by helping them share events with friends. Before founding InGo, he spoke globally on using social media to build grassroots movements, often recruiting hundreds of supporters in a day. He studied at Virginia Tech and interned in UK Parliament for Nigel Waterson. Michael is based in the Washington, D.C. area, where he leads InGo.
session_title: AI Can’t Create Trust. But It Can Scale It.

name: Nejc (Nates) Skoberne
bio: Nejc is CEO of Sellestial, the AI-powered data layer for HubSpot, processing millions of CRM records through governed pipelines. He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science and has built three tech companies at the intersection of AI and B2B operations. He teaches a hands-on AI for RevOps course focused on deploying AI that doesn’t break your CRM. A HubSpot Solutions Partner, he believes the real challenge isn’t making AI smarter, it’s making it safe enough to write to your system of record. Father of five, with a high tolerance for chaos.
session_title: How to Stop AI Agents from Making Stuff Up

name: Nida Ateeq
bio: Nida Ateeq is a revenue operations leader with 12+ years of experience across marketing, sales, and RevOps, working with B2B SaaS and enterprise organizations. She has led HubSpot CRM, reporting, and GTM system transformations for 100+ companies, influencing over $500K in pipeline. Nida specializes in building scalable, AI-ready revenue systems by improving data quality, CRM architecture, and cross-team alignment so teams can trust their metrics and decisions.
session_title: From Dashboards to Decisions: How to Build Revenue Reporting

name: Phil Vallender
bio: Phil Vallender is co-founder of Blend, a B2B marketing and growth consultancy focused on helping organizations build more predictable revenue through better strategy, structure, and execution. With a background in engineering and product management at Sony and more than a decade in B2B marketing, Phil brings technical discipline and business clarity to modern GTM challenges. He works with leadership teams to translate growth ambition into structured, measurable marketing systems. Phil also co-hosts the HubSpot Podcast Network show Demand Decoded and hosts Websites Decoded.
session_title: The Loop Unplugged: Inside Modern B2B Growth

name: Remington Begg
bio: Remington Begg is a CRM strategist and agency owner who has been a HubSpot partner since 2012. He works at the intersection of AI tools and B2B go-to-market strategy, helping marketing teams and founders build systems that grow smarter rather than just faster.
session_title: Running Loops: A Marketer's Guide to AI-Era Growth

name: Sam Anderson
bio: Sam Anderson is chief growth officer at Campaign Creators, an Elite HubSpot Solutions Partner, and former CEO of Origin 63. A longtime leader in the HubSpot ecosystem, she has helped more than 550 companies transform their customer journeys by unifying marketing, sales, and service data. Sam is known for helping marketing leaders turn customer experience insights into smarter segmentation, personalization, and lifecycle campaigns that drive measurable revenue growth.
session_title: Marketing's Secret Weapon: CX Signals That Drive Revenue

name: Sam Chandler
bio: Sam Chandler is the head of commercial customer success at Kustomer. Formerly of Zendesk, she has advised hundreds of companies—from startups to the Fortune 500—on GTM strategies that drive net revenue attention (NRR). Named a LinkedIn Top Voice, Sam is known for her engineering-led approach to growth—architecting AI systems that empower lean teams to match the impact of entire departments. She is the creator of the Growth Filter Index (GFI), a proprietary framework used to transform unstructured data into actionable signals; a system that most recently fueled a 47-point jump in NRR.
session_title: How We Increased NRR by 47 Points

name: Sam Lee
bio: Sam Lee is VP of pricing strategy and product operations at HubSpot, with more than 20 years of experience in B2B pricing strategy, monetization, and go-to-market operations. Previously, Sam spent four and a half years leading pricing strategy and operations at Snowflake, where he played a key role in shaping the company's consumption-based pricing model. He specializes in aligning pricing with product strategy, including emerging approaches to AI monetization.
session_title: Your Pricing Model Is a Customer Experience—Make It a Good One

name: Seán Reid
bio:
Seán is a recosgnied voice and thought leader within the customer success space. He has challenged modern CS metrics and looked to evolve the function to one that marries the customer obbessed mindset of strong CSM with the revenue driven impact of an AE.

His work on renewal probabilty scoring was nominated for Innovation of the Year at the Irish CX Awards 2025 and he has been listed by Favikon as one of the Top 10 Global Voices for Customer Experience. He started his career in HubSpot and has since led CS teams in Personio, Tive, and Supermetrics.
session_title: Predict Churn Before It Happens Without Health Scores

name: Sheena Collier
company: Boston While Black, The Collier Connection
company_normalized: boston while black, the collier connection
bio: Sheena Collier is a cultural strategist, founder, and community architect building belonging across cities, work, and identity. With 20+ years in partnerships and growth, she works at the intersection of culture and business. She is Founder and CEO of The Collier Connection and founder of Boston While Black, a platform helping cities and organizations attract and support Black talent. A Boston Magazine 100 Most Influential Bostonian, she is a Spelman and Harvard graduate and serves on the Massachusetts Convention Center Authority board.
session_title: The Belonging Economy: Marketing’s New Competitive Advantage

name: Shelby Kehr
bio: Shelby Kehr is a manager of analytics engineering at HubSpot, where she leads the marketing analytics engineering team focused on turning complex data into actionable insights across marketing and strategy and ops. With ten years in data and six at HubSpot, Shelby has held roles spanning data analyst, analytics engineer, and people leader, giving her a unique perspective on data systems and business impact. Shelby is passionate about making data feel approachable and empowering teams to work with it confidently, so insights drive smarter decisions and better systems.
session_title: Building a Data System That Powers Growth

name: Sophie Higgs
bio: Sophie leads HubSpot’s Smart CRM team, responsible for building the unified data foundation that powers HubSpot’s AI-powered customer platform. Her team focuses on making complex data simple, trustworthy, and actionable for growing companies around the world.
session_title: How Smart CRM Powers the Agentic Customer Platform

name: Steph Crigler
bio: Steph Crigler is a lead product marketer at HubSpot, helping to shape HubSpot's Loop Marketing framework. With nearly a decade in martech, Steph has partnered closely with product and Flywheel teams to launch products and frameworks that help marketers adapt and execute their strategies.
session_title: Running Loops: A Marketer's Guide to AI-Era Growth

name: Sumedha Rai
bio: Sumedha Rai is a New York-based senior researcher, AI architect and thought leader with more than a decade of experience turning complex AI into practical, ethical impact across fintech, healthcare, and policy. She leads enterprise AI deployments at Domyn, a responsible AI firm, and previously led ML for fraud-detection initiatives at a Acorns Grow, micro-investments company. A visiting researcher at NYU, her work focuses on misinformation and bias in large language models. She has been featured in Microsoft News and Business Insider.
session_title: The Death of the Natural Closer in Sales

name: Ty Heath
bio: Ty Heath is global director, thought leadership GTM strategy at LinkedIn and co-founder of The B2B Institute, LinkedIn's think tank for building famous B2B brands. With 20-plus years at Google, IBM, and Nestlé, she bridges marketing science and go-to-market strategy, turning research into frameworks teams can actually use. A two-time Olympic Trials qualifier in the 800 meters, Ty brings the same discipline to brand building that she brings to the track.
session_title: The B2B Credibility Code

name: Zachary Swetz
bio: Zachary Swetz is a group product manager at HubSpot.
session_title: How Data Agent Fuels Your Growth Engine With Context and Signals

name: Alex Rainford
bio: Alex Rainford is a product marketing manager at HubSpot.
session_title: What's New in Sales Hub

name: Tom Chen
bio: Tom Chen is Chief Product Officer at Aircall, where he leads product strategy focused on helping sales and support teams deliver better customer conversations with AI. He previously served as CPO at Humu and held product leadership roles at Instacart, Lyft (Lyft Business), and Amazon Web Services.
session_title: Turn Conversations Into Automated Revenue Workflows
